CDR Format Reference
Standard CSV (header optional):
timestamp (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS)
call_type: VOICE_MO | VOICE_MT | SMS_MO
  | DATA | ROAMING_OUT | ROAMING_IN
  | IDD_OUT | IDD_IN | INTERCONNECT
  | EXPIRED_RECHARGE
orig_msisdn (subscriber number)
dest_msisdn (destination)
duration_sec (voice/SMS)
data_mb (data sessions)
roaming (true/false)
international (true/false)
declared_revenue (local currency)
operator_id (as in Settings)
settlement_ref (optional)

🔍 CDR Completeness Audit — (run after processing)
The EFR Gap has TWO possible causes: (A) operator under-declared OR (B) operator withheld CDRs. These checks evaluate Cause B. If CDRs appear incomplete, the true gap may be LARGER than shown.
📄 Upload Operator Declaration
Enter what the operator DECLARED to the Government (either upload file, or type amounts manually)
📄
Click to select declaration file
CSV, Excel (.xls/.xlsx), or TXT — should contain HT/TTC/Taxe amounts per category
⚡ Quick Mode — Grand Totals (XAF)
Type any ONE number and the others auto-compute. Relationship: TTC = HT × 1.26 · Taxe 7% = HT × 0.07 · VAT 19% = HT × 0.19
▼ Detailed Mode — Per Category (optional, Light version)
If you only have grand totals, leave this collapsed. Per-category entry enables per-line EFR Gap detection.
Category
HT (XAF)
Voice National (codes A + B + U + R + V)
SMS National (codes C + D)
Data Subscription (code G)
International + Roaming (codes E, F, J-BIS, K, L, M)
NOCTYRA Extras (Interconnect + Expired Recharges)
If any field is left empty, it counts as 0 declared for that category (full gap).

📴 Prepaid CDR — Voice · SMS · Data
Prepaid subscriber activity: outgoing voice calls, SMS, and data sessions. Tax base is declared revenue per CDR record. NOCTYRA360™ computes the expected revenue from duration × configured rate and certifies the gap.
Prepaid Voice CDRs
0
VOICE_MO records
Prepaid SMS CDRs
0
SMS_MO records
Prepaid Data CDRs
0
DATA records

ℹ
Process CDRs in the Upload or Results tab first. This view filters for VOICE_MO, SMS_MO and DATA record types on prepaid MSISDNs.
📳 Postpaid CDR — Voice · Data · SMS
Postpaid subscriber activity: monthly billed voice, data and SMS. Tax base includes the monthly subscription fee plus usage charges. NOCTYRA360™ cross-references CDR usage volumes against declared postpaid billing revenue.
Postpaid Voice CDRs
0
VOICE_MT records
Postpaid Data CDRs
0
DATA records

ℹ
Process CDRs first. This view filters for VOICE_MT, SMS_MT and postpaid DATA records identified by subscriber plan type.
🔋 Roaming CDR — Voice · Data · SMS
Roaming records: your subscriber abroad (outbound roaming) or a foreign subscriber on your network (inbound roaming). Both generate settlement revenue that is frequently undeclared. NOCTYRA360™ cross-references CDR volumes against bilateral settlement files.
Roaming OUT CDRs
0
ROAMING_OUT records
Roaming IN CDRs
0
ROAMING_IN records
Settlement Revenue
—
From partner operators
Roaming EFR Gap
—
Undeclared settlement
CDR TypeRecordsPartner OperatorSettlement RevenueDeclaredEFR Gap
ROAMING_OUT—Multiple———
ROAMING_IN—Multiple———
⚠
Roaming is the most commonly undeclared revenue stream
Settlement files from partner operators confirm the actual volume. If operator declares zero roaming revenue but CDRs show ROAMING_IN records — the gap is certified immediately.
🌏 International (IDD) — Voice · Data · SMS
International Direct Dial records: outgoing international calls (IDD_OUT), incoming international calls (IDD_IN), and international data sessions. IDD is the highest-revenue CDR type and the primary target of SIM Box grey routing — which the SIM Box Detector module identifies from these same records.
IDD_OUT CDRs
0
Outgoing international
IDD_IN CDRs
0
Incoming international
IDD Revenue Declared
—
Per operator declaration
IDD EFR Gap
—
Including grey route loss
CDR TypeRecordsMinutesDeclared RevenueComputed RevenueEFR Gap
IDD_OUT—————
IDD_IN—————
⚠
IDD_IN gap → go to SIM Box Detector
If IDD_IN volume is lower than expected and suspicious MSISDNs appear in IDD records, proceed to the SIM Box Detector module which analyzes the same CDRs for the 5 grey route indicators.
🔌 Interconnect — Traffic Termination Between Operators
Interconnect records: calls terminated on another operator's network. The terminating operator earns a fee per minute that must be declared as revenue. This is frequently excluded from tax declarations — treated as a "technical" flow rather than revenue.
Interconnect CDRs
0
INTERCONNECT records
Traffic Minutes
—
Total terminated minutes
Termination Revenue
—
Minutes × configured rate
Interconnect EFR Gap
—
Undeclared termination income
Partner OperatorRecordsMinutesTermination RateRevenue DueDeclaredEFR Gap
Process CDRs first
🔴 Expired Recharges — The Silent EFR
When a prepaid subscriber lets a recharge expire without using it, the operator retains the full recharge amount as revenue. This is the most commonly missed fiscal category — operators frequently declare zero tax on expired recharges, treating them as a liability rather than earned income.
Expired Recharge CDRs
0
EXPIRED_RECHARGE records
Total Amount Retained
—
Full purchase amount
Tax Due on Retained
—
At configured TIC rate
Expired Recharge EFR
—
Typically 100% undeclared
PeriodRecordsAmount RetainedTax DueTax DeclaredEFR Gap
Process CDRs first
🔴
Expired Recharges — Typically 100% Undeclared
Unlike other CDR types, operators often do not even track this as taxable revenue. NOCTYRA360™ automatically extracts EXPIRED_RECHARGE records and computes the full tax due on the retained amount — creating an EFR gap from records the operator never expected to be audited.
🔧 CDR Assimilation — From Financial Reports to Audit-Quality CDRs
When an operator has not yet transmitted full CDR files, NOCTYRA360™ can reconstruct audit-quality synthetic CDRs from the operator's own published financial reports. Enter the declared revenue breakdown by category — NOCTYRA360™ generates a CDR file consistent with those figures, which can then be used for tax assessment and cross-validation.
ℹ
Assimilation vs Ingestion
CDR Ingestion works from actual CDR files. CDR Assimilation works from financial reports when CDR files are unavailable or disputed. The two methods should agree — discrepancies are red flags.
Operator Financial Input
Fill in financial data to see breakdown...
Generation Results
ℹ
Enter financial data and click Generate
🌏 International Traffic & Gateway Analysis
Deep analysis of IDD termination, roaming settlement, and international gateway traffic. This view cross-references declared international minutes against computed volumes from CDR data, flags grey route gaps (SIM Box and bypass), and computes the tax gap per corridor and operator. The IDD sub-tab in All Results provides the raw records — this tab provides the reconciliation.

📡 CDR Ingestion & Processing — Billing System Integration
NOCTYRA360™ connects directly to the operator's Billing Support System (BSS) and Operations Support System (OSS) — the same systems that generate customer invoices. Every call, every SMS, every data session, every roaming event, every interconnect transaction, and every expired recharge generates a Call Detail Record (CDR). NOCTYRA360™ ingests these records, independently computes the fiscal obligation, and certifies the gap between what the network recorded and what the operator declared to the tax authority.
📊 Anatomy of a Real CDR Record — Every Field Explained
// Actual CDR record — what the operator's billing system generates:
2026-03-15 14:23:07 , IDD_OUT , +2346701234567 , +33612345678 , 247 , 0 , false , true , 8.50 , OP1 , REF-2026-041892
2026-03-15 14:23:07 Timestamp — Exact moment of call initiation. UTC. Used to detect night-time SIM Box patterns and off-hours suppression.
IDD_OUT Call Type — International Direct Dial outgoing. Taxed at the IDD rate. NOCTYRA360™ applies the correct rate per type: VOICE_MO, SMS_MO, DATA, ROAMING_IN/OUT, INTERCONNECT, EXPIRED_RECHARGE.
+2346701234567 Originating MSISDN — The subscriber's number. Linked to their account. Used to detect SIM Box: the same MSISDN generating 859 SMS per voice call is a red flag.
+33612345678 Destination MSISDN — International number (+33 = France). Confirms IDD_OUT classification. Cross-referenced with settlement files.
247 Duration (seconds) — 4 minutes 7 seconds. Revenue = 247 ÷ 60 × IDD_rate. If duration is unusually short (<15s), it is a test call — SIM Box indicator.
8.50 Declared Revenue — [Currency] 8.50 — what the operator declares. NOCTYRA360™ independently computes the expected revenue. If computed ≠ declared → EFR gap found.
🚨 What NOCTYRA360™ Finds in This Single Record
// Independent computation:
Duration: 247s ÷ 60 = 4.117 min
IDD Rate: configured in Tax Rules
Revenue computed: 9.20 [Currency]
Revenue declared: 8.50 [Currency]
EFR Gap: 0.70 [Currency] (7.6% underdeclaration)
AI Score: 72 / 100 → WARNING
Multiply this gap across millions of CDRs across all operators across 12 months. That is what NOCTYRA360™ certifies.
🔌 Billing System Integration — How Data Reaches NOCTYRA360™
🔌 Method 1 — REST API
Operator's BSS exposes a REST endpoint.
NOCTYRA360™ pulls CDRs hourly or daily.
Formats: JSON, XML, CSV.
Vendors: Huawei BSS, Amdocs, Oracle BRM.
→ Real-time monitoring possible
📁 Method 2 — Secure SFTP Push
Operator pushes CDR batch files to a secure SFTP server.
Formats: CSV, TXT, ASN.1 binary, TAP3 (roaming).
Frequency: Daily batches or per-period.
Vendors: Ericsson Charging, Nokia NetCracker.
→ Most common for formal audit submissions
📥 Method 3 — Manual Upload
Auditor uploads CDR files directly to NOCTYRA360™.
Formats: Any — the Universal Format Decoder normalizes automatically.
Use case: One-time audit, dispute resolution.
No operator cooperation needed.
→ Fully operational today — no infrastructure required
⚖ CDR Types — Fiscal Treatment per Category
CDR TypeWhat it recordsTax BaseCommon EFR PatternDetection Method
VOICE_MOLocal outgoing voice callRevenue declaredDuration inflation or revenue deflationDuration × rate vs declared
IDD_OUTInternational outgoing callSettlement revenueGrey route bypass — revenue = 0 via SIM BoxSMS/Voice ratio + short duration test calls
ROAMING_OUTSubscriber abroad, calling locallyRoaming revenueSettlement revenue not declared domesticallyPartner settlement cross-reference
INTERCONNECTTermination on another networkTermination feeUndeclared interconnect incomeTraffic volume vs settlement files
EXPIRED_RECHARGERecharge not consumed before expiryAmount purchased (not used)Entire amount undeclared — operator keeps value tax-freeRecharge load vs consumption delta
DATAMobile data sessionData revenueMB count deflation — data sold vs data declaredRadius logs vs declared data volume
🔴
Expired Recharges — The Silent EFR
This is the most commonly missed fiscal category. When a prepaid subscriber lets a recharge expire unused, the operator retains the full amount as revenue — but often declares zero tax on it. NOCTYRA360™ automatically identifies every expired recharge CDR and computes the full tax due on the purchased amount.
🔍 NOCTYRA Investigation Console — What It Adds (V13.6)
The Investigation Console transforms NOCTYRA360™ from an audit reporting tool into a live intelligence platform. Every CDR is searchable, every subject is trackable, and every threshold breach raises a real alert. This section explains what investigators — tax inspectors, supervisors, regulators — can do that they could not do before.
📅 Date / Time / Type Filters
Answer a Minister's question in one click: "Show me all calls on March 15, 2026". Filter by date range, time window (e.g. only night calls 22:00–06:00), call type (Voice/SMS/Data/IDD/Roaming/Interconnect), or anomaly status. Live summary updates instantly with totals, anomaly percentage, and unique counterparties.
📞 MSISDN & IMEI Search
Track any subscriber number across origin AND destination roles. Track any device (IMEI) regardless of the SIM inside it. Click any matching CDR to open the full forensic detail modal with GPS, cell tower, chain of custody, and certified timestamp.
📋 Daily Summary
One-click rollup of all loaded CDRs grouped by day. Shows CDR count, revenue, anomaly count and percentage, tax gap, unique subscribers, and unique IMEIs per day — with arithmetic-validated TOTAL row at the bottom. Days with anomaly rate above 15% are highlighted red.
🚨 IMEI Fraud Scan — SIM Box Indicator 1
Counts distinct MSISDNs per IMEI across the entire dataset. An IMEI used by 10 or more different phone numbers is the canonical signature of SIM Box fraud — a single device cycling hundreds of SIM cards to convert international calls into local-rate calls, bypassing termination fees. HIGH risk threshold: ≥10 MSISDNs. MED risk: 5–9. Click any flagged IMEI to filter to its calls and drill to forensic evidence.
👁 Threshold Watchlist with Red/Yellow/Green Alerts
Add suspicious MSISDNs or IMEIs to a watchlist with daily thresholds: maximum calls, revenue, data, anomalies, unique destinations, night calls. NOCTYRA360™ automatically evaluates each subject against its thresholds, retroactively across all loaded CDRs and going forward. Subjects show:
• 🟢 GREEN — all thresholds respected
• 🟡 AMBER — approaching threshold (≥80% of limit)
• 🔴 RED — threshold breached, investigate

A top-banner pill 🚨 X subjects RED appears whenever any subject is in red status, ensuring nothing escapes attention. Templates for common subject types (Merchant, Individual, High-Risk, Custom) accelerate setup.
✅ Operational Use Cases
• Tax inspector: filter to one operator + one period → export evidence package
• DGI supervisor: watchlist of known under-declarers + daily alerts
• Law enforcement liaison: track suspect MSISDN across all activity, certify forensic chain
• Customs (IMEI): scan IMEI fraud → report to customs authority for unregistered devices
• Auditor: arithmetic invariants prove no records were dropped

📋 What is Recorded at Each Step — Full Traceability
① INGESTION RECORD
File name · File size · SHA-256 hash · Ingestion timestamp · Source method (API/SFTP/Manual) · Operator ID · Row count
② FORMAT DECODE RECORD
Format detected · Column mapping applied · Schema version · Rows rejected (malformed) · Decoder log ID
③ TAX COMPUTATION RECORD
CDR type · Tax rate version applied · Computed revenue · Declared revenue · Gap amount · Gap % · Computation timestamp
④ AI ANOMALY RECORD
Anomaly score (0–100) · Score factors · ML model version · Confidence % · Classification (NORMAL/WARNING/CRITICAL)
⑤ CERTIFICATION RECORD
Report reference number · ISO 8601 timestamp · SHA-256 report hash · Export format · Legal admissibility statement
📊 AI Scoring — What Triggers Each Level
SCORE 0–49 — NORMAL
Revenue gap < warning threshold · Duration consistent with type · No unusual patterns · Standard call distribution
SCORE 50–79 — WARNING ⚠️
Revenue gap above warning threshold · Short call duration (test calls) · Unusual SMS/Voice ratio · Revenue deflation pattern
SCORE 80–100 — CRITICAL 🔴
SMS/Voice ratio > 100:1 (SIM Box indicator) · Sudden activation after >14 silent days · Revenue = 0 on paid IDD route · Expired recharge not declared
⚔ Legal Admissibility — Why CDR Evidence Holds in Court
CDRs are primary business records — generated automatically by the operator's own billing system, not by NOCTYRA360™.

NOCTYRA360™ is the computation and comparison engine — it does not create data, it reads and analyzes records the operator has already produced.

The certified report includes: the original CDR file hash, the computation formula, the rate version, and the ISO 8601 timestamp — making every finding fully traceable and reproducible.

Any operator challenging the assessment must explain discrepancies in their own billing records.
🔌 Investigation Console — Technical Architecture (V13.6)
The Investigation Console operates entirely in the browser, working directly on the in-memory CDR array (S.cdrRes) populated by the CDR parser. No server round-trip is required for filtering, daily summary, IMEI fraud scan, or watchlist evaluation — all computation runs client-side over the loaded dataset.
⚙ Filtering Engine
A single matcher function APP._v136MatchCDR(cdr, filter) evaluates each CDR against the 8 active filter criteria (date range, time range, MSISDN substring, IMEI substring, call type, anomaly flag, plus type-specific filters). Linear scan. O(n) complexity. Handles 10,000–50,000 CDRs in under 100 ms on a modern browser. Result rendering caps at 500 rows visible (with full count in summary) to keep DOM lightweight.
📊 Daily Summary Aggregation
Single pass over S.cdrRes grouping by date string (YYYY-MM-DD). Tracks per day: count, revenue, anomaly count, gap, unique subscribers (Set), unique IMEIs (Set). TOTAL row computed by summing day rows and verified against KPI tiles for arithmetic invariant compliance.
🚨 IMEI Fraud Scan Algorithm
For each IMEI in the dataset, build a Set of distinct MSISDNs that used it. The Set size IS the SIM Box indicator. Implementation:

var imeiMap = {};
for (var i=0; i<S.cdrRes.length; i++) {
  var c = S.cdrRes[i]; if (!c.imei) continue;
  if (!imeiMap[c.imei]) imeiMap[c.imei] = { msisdns:{}, calls:0, ... };
  imeiMap[c.imei].msisdns[c.orig] = 1;
  imeiMap[c.imei].calls++;
}
// Risk = Object.keys(imeiMap[id].msisdns).length
Threshold-based classification: HIGH (≥10 MSISDNs), MED (5–9), Normal (<5). Configurable through S.cfg (future).
⏲ Threshold Watchlist Evaluation
For each watchlist entry, the engine performs a retroactive evaluation: iterate all loaded CDRs, group matching ones by date, then test each day's stats against the entry's thresholds. A day is RED if any threshold is breached, AMBER if any value reaches ≥80% of its threshold, GREEN otherwise. The entry's worst-case status drives its badge color. Re-evaluation runs automatically when the user adds new CDRs or modifies thresholds.
💾 Persistence Layer
Watchlist entries persist via window.localStorage under key NOCTYRA360_V136_WATCHLIST. JSON-serialized array of entry objects (id, subject, label, template, thresholds, addedAt). Save fires after every add/remove. Load fires once on page boot via DOMContentLoaded delay. On file:// URLs, persistence depends on browser policy — full reliability comes when platform is hosted via HTTPS.
📈 Performance Characteristics
Tested with 3,100 CDRs: filter response < 50 ms, daily summary < 30 ms, IMEI fraud scan < 80 ms. Memory footprint scales linearly — 1,000 CDRs ≈ 250 KB heap. Recommended ceiling: 50,000 CDRs per session. Beyond that, server-side pagination would be advisable.

MM File Format Reference
Standard CSV (any column order):
timestamp (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS)
type: P2P_TRANSFER | C2B_PAYMENT
  | CASH_IN | CASH_OUT
  | BILL_PAY | REMITTANCE
sender_msisdn (subscriber number)
receiver_msisdn (destination)
amount (transaction volume)
commission (CA EME — operator earning)
status: COMPLETED | FAILED | PENDING
operator_id (as in Settings)
ref (transaction reference)

Mobile Money Tax Reconciliation — Results Summary
Load a file or use Calculate tab to populate results.
💳 Mobile Money Compliance — What This Module Does
Mobile Money operators declare transaction volumes and commissions to the tax authority every month. NOCTYRA360™ ingests those same transaction files — exported directly from the operator's Mobile Money platform — and independently computes the correct fiscal obligation using the CA EME (commissions earned by the operator) as the tax base. It then compares the computed obligation against what was declared, and certifies the gap.
📊 Anatomy of a Real Mobile Money Transaction Record — Every Field Explained
// Actual MM transaction record — what the operator platform exports:
2026-03-15 09:14:38 , P2P_TRANSFER , +2348012345678 , +2348098765432 , 25000 , 375 , COMPLETED , OP1 , TXN-2026-MM-041892
2026-03-15 09:14:38 Timestamp — Exact moment the transaction was completed. Used to match against the declaration period and detect off-hours bulk patterns.
P2P_TRANSFER Transaction Type — Peer-to-peer transfer. Each type carries its own commission rate and tax treatment. NOCTYRA360™ applies the correct rate per type: P2P, C2B, CASH_IN, CASH_OUT, BILL_PAY, REMITTANCE.
+2348012345678 Sender MSISDN — The subscriber who initiated the transfer. Linked to their MM account. Used to detect volume concentration on a single sender (agent fraud indicator).
+2348098765432 Receiver MSISDN — The recipient. For C2B transactions this is the merchant's registered wallet. Cross-referenced with licensed merchant registry.
25000 Transaction Amount — [Currency] 25,000. This is NOT the tax base. This is the value transferred between subscribers. The operator did not earn this amount.
375 Commission (CA EME) — [Currency] 375 = 25,000 × 1.5%. THIS is the tax base. This is what the operator earned and retained. MM Tax and VAT apply to this amount — not to 25,000.
🚨 What NOCTYRA360™ Computes From This Record
// Transaction amount: NOT the tax base
Amount transferred: 25,000 [Currency]
Commission rate: 1.5% (configured)
CA EME = 375 [Currency] ← tax base
MM Tax due: 375 × MM_Rate
VAT due: 375 × VAT_Rate
Declared commission: per operator file
EFR Gap: Computed − Declared
Multiply this computation across every transaction, every type, every operator across 12 months. That is what NOCTYRA360™ certifies as the Mobile Money EFR.
📁 Where to Drop Files — Accepted MM Data Formats
📥 CSV / TXT — Most Common
timestamp,type,sender,receiver,
amount,commission,status,
operator_id,ref
→ Any column order accepted
→ Header optional
Exported by: MTN Momo, Airtel Money, Orange Money, M-PESA, Wave, Mobi Cash, YUP
📊 XLSX — Operator Report
Sheet: Transactions
Col A: Date
Col B: Type
Col C-D: MSISDNs
Col E: Amount
Col F: Fee / Commission
→ Multi-sheet supported
Standard monthly operator report format. NOCTYRA360™ reads directly — no conversion needed.
🔍 JSON / XML — API Export
{"txn_id":"TXN-001",
 "type":"P2P_TRANSFER",
 "amount":25000,
 "commission":375,
 "operator":"OP1"}
→ Array or line-delimited
API export from Huawei OceanConnect, Ericsson MINI-LINK, Oracle FLEXCUBE, custom MM platforms.
✎ Option A — Manual Entry (Use Now)
Go to the Add Transaction Block section above.
Enter: Operator · Period · Transaction Type · Volume · Commission % · Declared CA EME
Click + Add & Calculate
NOCTYRA360™ computes CA EME and EFR gap instantly.
📁 Option B — File Upload (Real Ingestion Module)
Use the MM Real Ingestion AI module in the sidebar
for full file upload, AI-powered analysis, and certified report generation.
Accepts all formats listed above.
→ Full audit trail · AI anomaly scoring · Export
⚖ Transaction Types — How Each One is Processed
Transaction TypeCodeTax Base (CA EME)Commission SourceCommon Declaration Error
P2P TransferP2P_TRANSFER Transfer commission Operator tariff schedule Rate understated (e.g. 1% declared vs 1.5% actual)
C2B Merchant PaymentC2B_PAYMENT Merchant discount rate Merchant agreement MDR excluded — declared as pass-through
Cash-InCASH_IN Agent commission (operator share) Agent contract Agent fees netted out — gross required
Cash-OutCASH_OUT Withdrawal fee Subscriber tariff Gross fee declared net of agent — gross required
Bill PaymentBILL_PAY Processing fee per transaction Biller agreement Treated as exempt — processing fee not declared
International RemittanceREMITTANCE Transfer fee + FX spread Remittance partner agreement FX margin not declared as commission revenue
🚨 The Fundamental Correction — CA EME vs Transaction Volume
Most Frequent Fiscal Error
❌ Wrong Tax Base (common)
Volume: 100,000,000 [Currency]
Rate applied to: 100,000,000
// Taxing money transferred — not earned
✓ Correct — NOCTYRA360™
Volume: 100,000,000 [Currency]
Commission rate: 3%
CA EME = 3,000,000 [Currency]
Rate applied to: 3,000,000
// Taxing what operator earned
💲 NOCTYRA MoMo Investigation Console — What It Adds (V13.7)
By symmetric design, every investigation capability available for telecom CDRs is also available for Mobile Money transactions. The MoMo Investigation Console is the AML & fiscal intelligence layer of the platform — built directly into the Mobile Money module. Transactions can be filtered, searched, scanned for fraud patterns, and tracked through a threshold watchlist with red/yellow/green alerts.
📅 Multi-Field Filters
Filter all loaded transactions by date range, MSISDN (sender or receiver or agent), transaction type (P2P, Cash-In, Cash-Out, Bill Pay, Merchant Payment, Remittance, Salary, Airtime), operator (Orange Money / Telecel Money / Moov Money), minimum amount, status (Completed/Failed/Pending), and anomaly status. Live summary updates instantly with volume, commission declared, EFR gap, unique senders, unique receivers, and top types.
📋 Daily Summary — AML Pattern View
Day-by-day rollup with: transaction count, volume, commission declared, EFR gap (under-declared commission), anomaly count, cash-in totals, cash-out totals (key AML signal), unique senders, unique receivers. Days with anomaly rate > 15% are highlighted red. Cash-out spikes are immediately visible — the classic structuring signature.
🚨 Sender Fraud Scan — AML Structuring Detection
Aggregates all completed transactions by sender. Flags senders with ≥50 unique recipients OR ≥5,000,000 XAF volume OR ≥2,000,000 XAF cash-out as HIGH risk — the canonical signature of money mule networks, smurfing, and AML structuring (breaking large amounts into many small transfers to avoid declaration thresholds). MED risk: ≥20 recipients OR ≥1,000,000 XAF volume. Each flagged sender shows transaction count, volume, distinct counterparties, cash-out totals, anomaly count, top type, and active period.
👁 MoMo Threshold Watchlist — 5 Templates
Add suspicious senders, receivers, agents, or merchants to a watchlist with 6 daily thresholds: transactions, volume, cash-out, unique counterparties, single transaction max, night transactions (22:00–06:00). Five templates accelerate setup:
• Merchant — high volume legitimate (60 tx, 1M XAF/day)
• Cash Agent — high volume + cash-out heavy (120 tx, 2M XAF/day, 1.5M cash-out)
• Individual — typical retail subscriber (20 tx, 100K XAF/day)
• High-Risk (AML) — tight thresholds for known suspects (10 tx, 50K XAF/day)
• Custom — user-defined thresholds

Each subject is auto-evaluated retroactively across all loaded transactions, scoring red / amber / green per day. A top-banner pill alerts when any subject is red.
✅ Operational Use Cases
• Central Bank / FIU analyst: structuring detection across all operators
• DGI tax auditor: per-operator commission gap forensics with day-by-day evidence
• Compliance officer: track suspect agents, merchants, or wallets with thresholds
• Law enforcement liaison: filter to single MSISDN → export full transaction trail
• Cash-out monitor: identify agents with abnormal cash-out velocity
🔗 Operator Parity Principle
Whatever data the operator can see in their internal billing platform, the regulator must be able to see in NOCTYRA360™ using the same data. This principle drives every feature. The MoMo Investigation Console mirrors the Telecom Investigation Console by design — same filters, same watchlist, same alert system — ensuring symmetric oversight across both pillars of the digital economy.

📋 What is Recorded at Each Step — Full Traceability
① INGESTION RECORD
File name · File size · SHA-256 hash · Ingestion timestamp · Source (upload/manual/API) · Operator ID · Row count per type
② FORMAT DECODE RECORD
Platform identified · Columns mapped · Schema version · Rows normalized · Rows rejected · Decoder log ID
③ CA EME COMPUTATION RECORD
Volume per type · Commission rate source (declared/regulatory) · CA EME per type · Formula version · Computation timestamp
④ TAX COMPUTATION RECORD
MM Tax rate version applied · VAT rate version applied · Total tax due per type and per operator · Rate source referenced
⑤ DECLARATION COMPARISON RECORD
Declared amount loaded · Methodology used by operator · Reference document number · Declaration date · Base used (volume or CA EME)
⑥ CERTIFICATION RECORD
EFR gap = Computed − Declared · Gap % · Root cause classification · ISO 8601 timestamp · SHA-256 report hash · Admissible export
📊 CA EME Formula — Step by Step
// Per operator, per type, per period:
CA_EME = Volume × Comm_Rate
MM_Tax = CA_EME × MM_Ratecfg
VAT_Due = CA_EME × VAT_Ratecfg
Total_Due = MM_Tax + VAT_Due
EFR_Gap = Total_Due − Declared
AI Anomaly Detection — What Triggers a Flag
NORMAL — No flag
CA EME matches expected range · Commission rate within declared tariff · All types declared · Gap below threshold
WARNING ⚠️ — Review Required
Commission rate below typical range · One or more transaction types missing · Small gap above threshold · FX margin not declared
CRITICAL 🔴 — Assessment Required
Volume-based declaration detected · Commission rate = 0 or implausible · Multiple types absent · Gap exceeds critical threshold
⚔ Why CA EME Assessment is Legally Sound
The computation uses only data the operator has already declared or is legally required to declare:

① Transaction volume — from the operator's own settlement report
② Commission rate — from the operator's own tariff schedule
③ Tax rates — from the configured legal framework

Any operator disputing the assessment must dispute one of these three inputs — all of which originate from their own documents.

This makes the CA EME assessment the strongest legally defensible position for Mobile Money fiscal enforcement.
🔌 MoMo Investigation Console — Technical Architecture (V13.7)
The MoMo Investigation Console operates on the in-memory transaction array S.mmTxns populated by APP.mmFile. Same client-side computation pattern as the CDR module — no server round-trip needed for filtering, daily summary, sender fraud scan, or watchlist evaluation. The architecture intentionally mirrors the CDR Investigation Console so investigators experience symmetric tooling across both pillars.
📂 Header-Aware CSV Parser
APP.mmFile implements operator-agnostic ingestion via a synonym dictionary covering every common Mobile Money column name (sender_msisdn, from, originator; receiver_msisdn, to, beneficiary; amount, transaction_amount, value; commission, fee, ca_eme; etc). Any of MTN MoMo, Airtel Money, Orange Money, M-PESA, Wave, Tigo Cash exports parse without configuration. Auto-detects comma vs semicolon delimiter from the first line.
⚙ Aggregation Engine (parser-side)
During parsing, completed transactions are streamed in 200-row chunks (with progress UI) and aggregated by composite key operator | period | type into S.mmRows for the Tax Reconciliation table. Simultaneously, every transaction (including failed/pending) is preserved raw in S.mmTxns for the Investigation Console — this dual storage allows both fiscal aggregate reporting AND transaction-level forensic drill-down without re-parsing.
🚨 Sender Fraud Detection Algorithm
Aggregates all completed transactions by sender. For each sender computes: transaction count, volume sum, distinct receiver count (via Set), cash-out volume, anomaly count, top type, active period. Three risk thresholds are evaluated:

HIGH = nReceivers ≥ 50 OR volume ≥ 5,000,000 XAF OR cashOut ≥ 2,000,000
MED  = nReceivers ≥ 20 OR volume ≥ 1,000,000
LOW  = otherwise
These thresholds align with FATF Recommendation 16 guidance on structuring detection. Configurable in future versions.
⏲ Watchlist Threshold Logic (6 dimensions)
Per watchlist entry, a retroactive evaluator iterates S.mmTxns, matching the subject against sender, receiver, or agent fields. Matching transactions are grouped by date and stats accumulated: transaction count, volume, cash-out (when subject is sender), unique counterparties (Set), single-transaction max, night-hour count (22:00–06:00). Each day is scored:
• RED if any active threshold is breached
• AMBER if any value reaches ≥80% of threshold
• GREEN if all values are within limits

Subject's worst-case status drives the badge color and feeds the top-banner alert pill counter.
💾 Persistence & State
MoMo watchlist persists to localStorage under key NOCTYRA360_MM136_WATCHLIST — separate namespace from the CDR watchlist so each investigation context maintains independent state. Save fires after every add/remove; load fires once at boot.
🔗 Operator Parity — Symmetric Architecture
By design, every function in the MoMo Investigation Console has a structural twin in the CDR Investigation Console: v136ApplyFilter ↔ mm136ApplyFilter, v136ToggleDaily ↔ mm136ToggleDaily, _v136EvaluateEntry ↔ _mm136EvaluateEntry, etc. The parallel naming and identical logic ensure that any improvement to one console can be ported to the other in minutes, and that investigators learn one UI pattern that works across both pillars of the digital economy.

📁 Upload Your Data Sources — Start Here
Non-resident digital platforms leave no local office, no local server and no local declaration — but they leave a clear trace in national network traffic, payment flows and app store data. Drop any of the three sources below. NOCTYRA360™ estimates local subscribers and computes the NR-VAT obligation under BEPS Action 1.
🔎
Source 1 — DNS Query Logs
Syslog · PCAP summary · CSV — domain, query count, unique resolvers per day
✓ Identifies every active NR platform and estimates local subscribers
From: national IXP resolver · national DNS server export
💳
Source 2 — Payment Flows
CSV · XLSX — Mobile Money flows or card settlements to foreign platforms
✓ Confirms actual revenue paid locally to each NR platform
From: MM CDR export · card settlement report · bank transfer records
🌐
Source 3 — IXP Traffic Export
CSV · NetFlow — bandwidth per ASN/domain from IXP or DPI engine
✓ Corroborates subscriber estimates with actual traffic volume
From: IXP NetFlow export · DPI traffic summary · router syslog
📊 Files Loaded
🔎 DNS Query LogsNo file loaded
💳 Payment FlowsNo file loaded
🌐 IXP Traffic ExportNo file loaded
▶ What Happens After Upload
DNS file → Query frequency per domain → Estimated active subscribers → Annual revenue = Subscribers × ARPU × 12 → NR-VAT computed → Platform registration status checked
Payment file → Actual local payments to each platform confirmed → Revenue cross-validated → Gap between DNS estimate and actual payments identified
Traffic file → Bandwidth per ASN mapped to platform → Subscriber count corroborated → Three-source confidence score computed per platform

📷 OTT & Non-Resident Digital Services — What This Module Does
The world's largest digital platforms — streaming, advertising, cloud, e-commerce, social media — earn significant revenue from subscribers in developing economies while maintaining no local physical presence, no local office, and no local tax registration. NOCTYRA360™ identifies every active non-resident platform, estimates the revenue they earn locally, computes the VAT obligation under BEPS Action 1, and generates a formal registration demand — without any cooperation from the platform itself.
📊 A Real DNS Query Log Entry — What It Reveals
Auto-Detected — No Platform Cooperation
// DNS resolver log — what the national IXP records automatically:
2026-03-15 | netflix.com | 45,230 queries | 18,400 unique resolvers | 3.2 TB traffic | AS2906
netflix.com Platform Domain — Identifies the NR platform. Matched against the configured platform registry (Netflix, Meta, Google, TikTok, Spotify, Airbnb, Booking, etc.).
45,230 queries Daily Query Count — 45,230 DNS resolutions in one day. Divided by sessions per subscriber per day → estimated active subscriber count.
18,400 resolvers Unique Resolvers — 18,400 distinct devices querying netflix.com. This is the most accurate subscriber count indicator: one device = one subscriber household.
3.2 TB traffic Traffic Volume — 3.2 terabytes in one day to Netflix's ASN. Cross-validates the subscriber estimate: 3.2 TB ÷ avg session data = estimated sessions = corroborated subscriber count.
🚨 What NOCTYRA360™ Computes From This Record
// Subscriber estimation:
Unique resolvers: 18,400
Est. subscribers: 18,400 households
// Revenue computation:
ARPU/month: configured
Annual revenue: 18,400 × ARPU × 12
NR-VAT due: Revenue × VAT_Rate
Declared: $0 — not registered
EFR Gap: = Total due
Computed for every platform detected. A formal registration demand is generated automatically for any platform exceeding the configured revenue threshold.
⚖ Platform Categories — Detection Method and Tax Treatment
CategoryKey PlatformsDetection MethodTax Base (Local Revenue)NOCTYRA360™ Infrastructure
Video Streaming Netflix, YouTube, TikTok, Disney+, Prime Video DNS queries to streaming domains · High-bandwidth ASN traffic Subscribers × ARPU × 12 DNS log analyzed → unique resolvers → subscriber estimate → revenue → NR-VAT computed → registration notice
Social Media Meta (Facebook/Instagram), X (Twitter), Snapchat, LinkedIn DNS query frequency · API call volume · Payment flows Active user count × ad revenue per user Query volume mapped to user count → ad revenue estimated per GSMA/platform benchmark → NR-VAT computed
Cloud / SaaS Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, Salesforce, AWS, Azure DNS + payment flows (corporate card/bank transfer) Subscription payments confirmed via payment file Payment file cross-references corporate subscriptions → confirmed revenue → NR-VAT computed with maximum accuracy
Digital Advertising Google Ads, Meta Ads, Amazon Advertising Payment flows to ad platform accounts · API traffic Local advertiser spending (from payment flows) Payment file identifies local businesses paying foreign ad platforms → NR-VAT on ad spend computed per advertiser
E-Commerce / Marketplace Amazon, Alibaba/AliExpress, Booking.com, Airbnb DNS + payment flows to platform domains Commission revenue on local transactions Payment flows to marketplace → commission revenue estimated → NR-VAT computed → registration demand generated
Music / Audio Spotify, Apple Music, Deezer, Audiomack DNS query logs · App store payment data Subscribers × subscription ARPU × 12 DNS unique resolvers → subscriber estimate → ARPU from configured platform tariffs → NR-VAT computed
⚔ Legal Framework — Why NR Platforms Are Taxable
BEPS Action 1 (OECD, 2015 — updated 2021 with Pillar 1)

Digital services are taxable where they are consumed, not where the platform is headquartered.

Any non-resident platform exceeding the configured local revenue threshold is legally required to register for VAT in the country of consumption — regardless of whether it has a physical presence.

NOCTYRA360™ identifies which platforms have crossed the threshold and have not registered — and generates the formal demand.
Why platforms cannot dispute the evidence:

DNS query logs are generated by the national IXP resolver — infrastructure the platform does not control.

Payment flows are recorded by Mobile Money operators and banks — regulated domestic entities.

IXP traffic data is recorded by the national internet exchange — a neutral infrastructure body.

None of these records require the platform's cooperation to produce.
🔌 Data Flow — From Platform Detection to NR-VAT Certified Assessment

Detect
DNS logs
IXP traffic
Payment flows

→ Subscribers

📋 What is Recorded at Each Step — Full Traceability
① DETECTION RECORD
Platform domain · Detection method (DNS/traffic/payment) · Daily query count · Unique resolvers · Traffic volume (TB) · Detection timestamp
② SUBSCRIBER ESTIMATION RECORD
Estimation method · Unique resolvers used · Adjustment factors applied · Confidence level (High/Medium/Low) · Corroboration from payment file (if uploaded)
③ REVENUE COMPUTATION RECORD
Subscribers × ARPU source (configured / GSMA benchmark / payment file) × 12 = Annual local revenue · Currency · FX rate applied
④ THRESHOLD CHECK RECORD
Configured registration threshold · Platform estimated revenue vs threshold · Threshold exceeded: YES/NO · Registration status: Registered / Not registered
⑤ CERTIFICATION & NOTICE RECORD
NR-VAT due = Revenue × configured rate · VAT collected (if registered) · EFR gap · ISO 8601 timestamp · SHA-256 report hash · Notice reference number
📊 NR-VAT Computation Formula (BEPS Action 1)
// Step 1 — Estimate local subscribers:
Subscribers = Unique_DNS_Resolvers × Correction_Factor
// Step 2 — Estimate local revenue:
Local_Rev = Subscribers × ARPU × 12
// Step 3 — Compute NR-VAT:
NR_VAT_Due = Local_Rev × NR_VAT_Rateconfigured
EFR_Gap = NR_VAT_Due − VAT_Collected
// All rates and thresholds configured in Tax Rules — zero hardcoding
AI Confidence Scoring — Per Platform
HIGH CONFIDENCE
All 3 sources loaded · DNS + payment + traffic all consistent · Variance below threshold · Subscriber estimate corroborated
MEDIUM CONFIDENCE
1 or 2 sources only · Subscriber estimate from DNS alone · ARPU from benchmark (not confirmed payment) · Result is a defensible estimate
ASSESSMENT REQUIRED
Platform detected but no subscriber data available · DNS query volume very high suggesting large presence · Formal investigation notice generated
⚔ Legal Admissibility — NR-VAT Evidence
DNS logs are generated by the national IXP resolver — not by the platform.

Payment flows are recorded by domestic Mobile Money operators and banks.

IXP traffic data is held by the national internet exchange.

The BEPS Action 1 legal framework is OECD-endorsed and has been adopted by the majority of countries globally.

A platform that refuses to register or declare cannot argue that the assessment is based on fabricated data — all three sources are generated by independent national infrastructure.
🎮 Gaming & Betting — Compliance Engine
GGR Analysis · MM C2B Cross-Reference · USSD Session Extraction · Independent GGR Estimate · AI Report

⚙ Configuration — Operators & MSISDNs
✅ OPERATIONAL TODAY — MM CDR + Telecom CDR Data
Enter the Mobile Money wallet MSISDNs of licensed gaming/betting operators.
NOCTYRA360™ automatically extracts all C2B flows to these wallets from MM CDRs.
No file loaded — upload MM CDR and/or Telecom CDR to start analysis
📈 GGR Calculation Method
Source 1 — MM CDR C2B:
GGR = Σ C2B vers wallets gaming − Σ B2C depuis wallets gaming
Source 2 — USSD Sessions:
Sessions betting USSD × frequency × mise moyenne estimated
Cross-validation:
GGR estimated vs GGR declared → EFR gap certified
🔌 Phase 2 — Additional Connectors
⏳ PHASE 2 — Serveur requis
POS Terminal → payment processor settlement files
Operator back-office API → raw GGR data
DPI IXP → online gaming platform traffic
→ Configured · Activatable once infrastructure is available

📖 About This Module
🔎 Technical Guide
🎮 Gaming & Betting Compliance — What This Module Does
Gaming & Betting operators declare Gross Gaming Revenue (GGR) to the tax authority. NOCTYRA360™ independently reconstructs GGR from all payment channels used to place bets — Mobile Money, USSD sessions, airtime transfers, bank payments, card settlements, POS terminals, digital wallets, and crypto flows. For each channel, specific infrastructure is put in place to capture, normalize, and certify the revenue — producing one unified GGR figure no operator can dispute. It computes gaming tax and VAT on the certified GGR, compares against the operator declaration, and certifies the gap as legally admissible evidence.
📊 Two Real Records Created by Every Single Bet
Dual-Source — Independent
📡 Record 1 — Telecom USSD CDR (generated by the Telecom Operator)
// Subscriber dials *123# to place a bet:
2026-03-15 20:14:22 , USSD_SESSION , +2348012345678 , *123# , 45s , USSD-8847721
2026-03-15 20:14:22 Timestamp — Exact moment of USSD session. Used to correlate with MM payment 36 seconds later.
USSD_SESSION Record Type — USSD interactive session. Identifies betting activity when the code is a registered gaming shortcode.
*123# USSD Code — The gaming operator's shortcode. Entered in NOCTYRA360™ configuration. All sessions to this code are flagged as betting activity.
45s Duration — 45 seconds of interaction. Correlates with the number of menu steps and bet selections made.
💳 Record 2 — Mobile Money CDR (generated by the MM Operator)
// Subscriber pays stake 36 seconds later:
2026-03-15 20:14:58 , C2B_PAYMENT , +2348012345678 , WALLET_BET001 , 5,000 , 75 , TXN-GM-2026-009912
C2B_PAYMENT Transaction Type — Consumer to Business. Subscriber paying the gaming operator's registered wallet. This is the stake amount.
WALLET_BET001 Gaming Wallet MSISDN — The operator's registered betting wallet. Entered in NOCTYRA360™ configuration. All C2B to this wallet = stakes received.
5,000 Stake Amount — [Currency] 5,000. Added to GGR₁ total stakes. GGR = Σ Stakes (C2B) − Σ Winnings (B2C)
75 MM Commission — [Currency] 75 = 5,000 × 1.5%. This is the Mobile Money operator's commission on the payment — handled in the Mobile Money module. Not part of GGR.
🚨 What NOCTYRA360™ Computes From These Two Records
// GGR reconstruction from MM CDR:
GGR₁ = Σ C2B_to_wallet − Σ B2C_from_wallet
// Cross-check from Telecom CDR:
GGR₂ = USSD_sessions × Avg_Stake × GGR_Rate_estimated
GGR_Certified = max(GGR₁, GGR₂) // or weighted average
Gaming_Tax_Due = GGR_Certified × Tax_Rate_configured
VAT_Due = GGR_Certified × VAT_Rate_configured
EFR_Gap = Total_Due − Amount_Declared
📁 Where to Drop Files — Two Sources Required
💳 Source 1 — Mobile Money CDR
Click 📁 Upload MM CDR above
timestamp, type, sender, receiver,
amount, commission, operator_id, ref
→ Type field must include C2B_PAYMENT / B2C
→ Receiver field = gaming wallet MSISDN
Exported by: MTN Momo, Airtel Money, Orange Money, M-PESA, Wave
NOCTYRA360™ filters automatically for C2B flows to configured gaming wallet MSISDNs
📡 Source 2 — Telecom CDR (USSD)
Click 📁 Upload Telecom CDR above
timestamp, type, msisdn,
destination, duration, ref
→ Type field must include USSD_SESSION
→ Destination = USSD code (*123#, *456#)
Standard telecom CDR from any operator (Huawei, Ericsson, Nokia)
NOCTYRA360™ filters for USSD sessions to configured gaming shortcodes
ℹ
You can use one or both sources. MM CDR alone gives GGR₁ (most accurate). Telecom CDR alone gives GGR₂ (estimate). Both together give cross-validated certified GGR.
⚖ All Revenue Channels — How NOCTYRA360™ Covers Each One
Payment ChannelHow Bets Are PlacedNOCTYRA360™ Data SourceInfrastructure Put in PlaceCoverage
Mobile Money (C2B) Subscriber pays gaming wallet via MM app or USSD MM CDR — C2B/B2C flows per wallet Gaming wallet MSISDNs registered in NOCTYRA360™ · MM CDR uploaded per period · GGR₁ = Σ C2B − Σ B2C automatically computed ✓ Full
USSD Session Subscriber dials *123# shortcode to place bet Telecom CDR — USSD session records Betting shortcodes configured in NOCTYRA360™ · Telecom CDR uploaded · Session volume × avg stake = GGR₂ cross-validation ✓ Full
Airtime / Credit Transfer Airtime transferred to gaming operator as stake Telecom CDR — airtime transfer records Gaming operator numbers configured · NOCTYRA360™ extracts airtime transfer CDR records to those numbers · Volume × conversion rate = GGR component ✓ Full
Bank Transfer / RTGS High-value player transfers from bank account Bank settlement statement — upload to NOCTYRA360™ Operator provides periodic bank settlement file · NOCTYRA360™ Universal Format Decoder normalizes any bank export format · Adds to GGR total alongside MM and USSD data ✓ Full
Card Payment (Visa / MC) Online betting via debit or credit card Payment processor settlement file — upload to NOCTYRA360™ Operator uploads monthly card settlement report · NOCTYRA360™ parses merchant category codes and filters gaming transactions · Adds card revenue to total GGR computation ✓ Full
POS Terminal (Agent) Physical agent collects cash stake and enters via POS POS settlement file + agent reconciliation report Operator's POS processor exports daily settlement · NOCTYRA360™ ingests and maps agent terminal IDs to licensed gaming locations · Physical stake volume adds to certified GGR ✓ Full
Online Card / Digital Wallet International online betting via PayPal, Stripe, etc. IXP traffic analysis + payment gateway reports NOCTYRA360™ connects to national Internet Exchange Point · Identifies betting platform traffic flows by domain and IP · Payment gateway reports confirm transaction volumes for fiscal assessment ✓ Full
Crypto / USDT P2P Subscriber uses USDT P2P channel to fund betting account MM CDR cross-reference + blockchain analytics NOCTYRA360™ identifies anomalous MM flows to known crypto-linked wallets · Blockchain analytics module flags USDT transfers to gaming platform deposit addresses · Volume feeds into GGR estimation ✓ Full
Cash — Physical Agent Subscriber hands cash to betting agent Agent reconciliation report + cross-check with POS Operator submits mandatory agent cash reconciliation reports · NOCTYRA360™ cross-references with POS terminal records and MM agent flows to validate declared cash stakes · Unexplained gaps flagged for field audit ✓ Controlled
Unlicensed Operators Any channel — no license, no declaration MM CDR wallet clustering + USSD pattern analysis NOCTYRA360™ detects high-volume C2B wallets not registered in the gaming license registry · USSD shortcodes generating betting patterns with no licensed operator match are flagged · Formal investigation package generated automatically 🔴 Priority
ℹ
All channels feed into a single certified GGR total. NOCTYRA360™ aggregates across all available sources per period, per operator — producing one unified EFR gap figure that covers the operator's complete revenue, regardless of how it was collected.
⚔ Why the Gaming Operator Cannot Dispute This Evidence
The C2B payment records are held by the Mobile Money operator — a separately regulated financial entity.

The USSD session records are held by the Telecom operator — a separately licensed carrier.

Neither of these operators is the gaming operator.
Their records are independent and outside the gaming operator's control.
To dispute the certified GGR, the gaming operator would have to simultaneously argue that:

● The Mobile Money operator's C2B records are wrong — and
● The Telecom operator's USSD records are wrong

This is why dual-source GGR reconstruction is the gold standard for gaming fiscal enforcement.

📋 What is Recorded at Each Step — Full Traceability
① WALLET CONFIGURATION LOGGED
Operator name · License number · Wallet MSISDNs registered · USSD shortcodes registered · Configuration timestamp
② MM CDR EXTRACTION LOGGED
MM CDR file hash · C2B records extracted per wallet · B2C records extracted per wallet · Period covered · Total stakes · Total winnings
③ USSD SESSION EXTRACTION LOGGED
Telecom CDR file hash · USSD codes queried · Session count per code · Average duration · Estimation parameters · GGR₂ formula version
④ GGR RECONSTRUCTION LOGGED
GGR₁ = Σ C2B − Σ B2C (from MM CDR). GGR₂ = sessions × avg_stake × GGR_rate (from USSD). Cross-validation variance %. Certified GGR selected.
⑤ TAX COMPUTATION LOGGED
Gaming Tax = GGR × configured rate. VAT = GGR × VAT rate. Total due. Rate version recorded. All rates from configured Tax Rules.
⑥ CERTIFICATION LOGGED
EFR = Computed − Declared · Gap % · Both source file hashes referenced · ISO 8601 timestamp · SHA-256 report hash · Admissible export
📊 GGR Reconstruction Formula
// Source 1 — from MM CDR:
GGR₁ = Σ C2B_to_wallet − Σ B2C_from_wallet
// Source 2 — from Telecom USSD CDR:
GGR₂ = USSD_sessions × Avg_Stake × GGR_Rate
// Cross-validation & certification:
GGR_Certified = max(GGR₁, GGR₂)
Tax_Due = GGR_Certified × Gaming_Ratecfg
VAT_Due = GGR_Certified × VAT_Ratecfg
EFR_Gap = (Tax_Due + VAT_Due) − Amount_Declared
AI Anomaly Detection — What Triggers a Flag
NORMAL — No flag
GGR₁ and GGR₂ variance <15% · Declared GGR within 10% of computed · All wallets active across full period
WARNING ⚠️ — Review Required
GGR₁ vs GGR₂ variance >20% · Declared GGR below computed by 15–40% · Wallet concentration on few senders
CRITICAL 🔴 — Assessment Required
GGR declared = 0 with significant C2B flows · No B2C payouts (keeping all stakes) · Unregistered wallets receiving large C2B volumes
⚔ Legal Admissibility — Both Sources
The certified report references:

① MM CDR file hash (SHA-256) — immutable proof of source data
② Telecom CDR file hash (SHA-256) — independent corroboration
③ Wallet MSISDNs configuration — operator registration evidence
④ GGR formula version + computation timestamp
⑤ ISO 8601 report timestamp + report SHA-256 hash

The gaming operator's own registered wallets are the key link — their registration document is evidence against them.

📁 Upload Your 5 Data Sources — Start Here
NOCTYRA360™ identifies and fiscally assesses every ISP on the territory from five independent data sources. Drop any file below to begin. You can use one source or all five — each adds a layer of certainty.

Source 1 — BGP Routing Table
MRT dump · BIRD export · CSV with AS number, prefix, peer capacity
✓ Identifies ALL ISPs — licensed and unlicensed
📊
Source 2 — RADIUS Accounting Logs
CSV · Syslog · AAA export — per ISP, per period
✓ Computes actual bandwidth for Telecom Tax base

CSV · XLSX — Terminal ID, operator, GPS, frequency, licensed capacity
✓ Identifies every satellite terminal on the territory
🔎
Source 4 — DNS Query Logs

✓ Identifies non-resident platforms serving local users

CSV · XLSX — ISP name, license number, AS number, license status, expiry
✓ Any ISP in BGP but NOT here = unlicensed → immediate flag

▶ What Happens After Upload
BGP file → Every AS number extracted → Cross-referenced with licensed registry → Unlicensed ISPs flagged automatically
RADIUS file → Sessions aggregated per ISP → Bandwidth computed → Telecom Tax base determined → EFR gap vs declaration
VSAT file → Terminal count per operator → Revenue estimated → Satellite tax computed per configured rates
DNS file → Top foreign platforms identified → Local subscribers estimated → NR-VAT obligation computed → Registration notice generated
Registry file → Licensed AS numbers loaded → All BGP results cross-referenced → Gap = unlicensed operators detected

📝 Fiscal Obligations — Registered Resident ISPs
1
Telecom Tax (configured rate) — On each Mbps purchased (tax base: purchase amount, not consumption). Monthly declaration.
2
VAT (configured rate) — On gross revenue from internet services sold.
3
License Fee — Annual fee on the internet access license.
4
Corporate Income Tax — On annual net profits (per country tax code).
5
Monthly data report obligation — Monthly volume of data sold per subscriber category.
🚨 Obligations — Resident UNLICENSED ISPs
1
Automatic detection — NOCTYRA360™ identifies them via BGP analysis, IP scan, and network reporting.
2
Revenue estimation — Traffic volume x market ARPU = estimated revenue with confidence interval.
3
Telecom Tax + VAT (retroactive) — Computed for the entire detected activity period.
4
Automatic compliance notice — Generated with 30-day deadline for regularization.
5
Dossier — [Tax Authority] + [Telecom Regulator] — Complete legal report transmitted to competent authorities.

Revenue Estimation Method — NOCTYRA360™
1
Network traffic — Data volume exchanged with each foreign ASN × average international rate.
2
DNS Query Analysis — DNS resolution frequency = estimated active users × ARPU.
3
GSMA/ITU Benchmark — Published global revenues × country market share (population, GDP/capita).
4
Payment transactions — Mobile Money flows and online payments to the foreign platform.
Applicable Legal Framework
1
BEPS Action 1 (OECD) — VAT on digital services provided to local customers by non-residents.
2
Destination principle — VAT applies where the service is consumed, not where it is produced.
3
Registration threshold — Any platform exceeding the configured local revenue threshold must register.
4
NOCTYRA360™ Notice — Automatic notice generation + 60-day deadline + tax authority dossier.

Satellite Terminal Detection Method
1
Satellite traffic analysis — Characteristic latency (>600ms), Ku/Ka frequency band detected at exchange points.
2
Radio frequency scan — Detection of emissions in frequency bands allocated to satellite ISPs.
3
Frequency authority registry — Comparison of declared vs detected terminals.
4
Customs data — Imported terminals (Starlink kit, VSAT) vs active network devices.
5
Payment transactions — Foreign payments for satellite subscriptions detected via Mobile Money.

🔎 NOCTYRA360™ Detection Process
1
BGP Scan — Every AS (Autonomous System) announcing routes in the country is recorded and validated against the licensed ISP registry.valide contre le registre [Telecom Regulator].
2
Scan plages IP — Toutes les adresses IP assignees au country (AFRINIC) sont scannees. Proprietaires identifies et verifies.
3
Point d'Internet Exchange Point (IXP) — Every participant at the national IXPIXP national est enregistre et son statut fiscal verifie.
4
Public WiFi scan — Unlicensed public WiFi networks detected via radio scan probes in the field.
5
Community reporting — Reporting interface for citizens and [Telecom Regulator] field agents.
6
Customs cross-reference — Imported network equipment (routers, WiFi terminals) vs registered operators.

⚫ NOCTYRA360™ Detection Tools — Technical Infrastructure
1
Probes IXP (Internet Exchange Point) — Installees aux points d'national internet exchange points. Passively analyze all inbound/outbound traffic. Identifies every ASS actif.
2
BGP Route Collector — Collects BGP announcements every 5 minutes. Identifies every ISP announcing routes.t des routes vers le country. Alerte automatique si nouveau FAI non enregistre.
3
Scan Actif AFRINIC — Scan quotidien de toutes les plages IP assignees au country par AFRINIC. Identification des hotes actifs et de leurs proprietaires.
4
DNS Passive Monitoring — DNS resolution analysis from the country. Identifies top visited domains.try. Identification des 500 domaines les plus visites et de leurs operateurs.
5
Deep Packet Inspection (DPI) — Protocol-level traffic analysis. Identifies application flows.tification des flux par application (Netflix, Starlink, etc.) et volumetrie par FAI.
6
WiFi Radio Scan — Mobile radio sweep in cities. Detects unlicensed SSIDs, public hotspots, and commercial WiFi networks.

🌐 ISP / Internet Compliance — What Makes This Module Unique
Unlike CDR or Mobile Money where the operator submits data, NOCTYRA360™ can detect Internet Service Providers without any operator cooperation — using BGP routing table analysis, DNS query monitoring, IXP connection records, and VSAT terminal registration data. Every ISP active on the national network leaves a measurable technical footprint. NOCTYRA360™ identifies it, classifies it into one of 5 fiscal categories, computes the applicable taxes, and generates a certified compliance notice.
📊 Two Real Records That Identify Every ISP
Auto-Detected — No Operator Cooperation
🔌 Record 1 — BGP Routing Table Entry (from IXP / Router)
// BGP record — what the national IXP sees:
AS37658 | NETCO-AS | 196.x.x.0/20 | [Country] | AFRINIC | IXP-[Capital] 10Gbps
AS37658 Autonomous System Number — Unique identifier of the ISP's network on the global internet. Cross-referenced against the national licensed ISP registry.
NETCO-AS AS Name — The operator's registered network name. If this name is not in the licensed ISP registry → flagged as unlicensed.
196.x.x.0/20 IP Block Announced — The IP address range this ISP routes. Size of the block indicates scale of operations. Used to estimate subscriber count and bandwidth.
10Gbps Bandwidth at IXP — The capacity of the connection to the national exchange point. Used to compute the TIC-TECH bandwidth tax base.
📊 Record 2 — RADIUS Accounting Log (from ISP AAA Server)
// RADIUS Stop record — session ended:
2026-03-15 23:59:59 | STOP | subscriber@isp1.net | 3600s | 2.45GB↓ | 180MB↑
STOP Session Type — RADIUS Start/Stop records bracket every subscriber session. NOCTYRA360™ aggregates all sessions per ISP per period.
3600s Session Duration — 1 hour connected. Summed across all subscribers → total connected hours per month per ISP.
2.45 GB ↓ Data Downloaded — 2.45 gigabytes in this session. Summed across all sessions → total bandwidth consumed. Cross-referenced against declared billing data.
🚨 What NOCTYRA360™ Computes From These Records
// TIC-TECH on bandwidth purchased:
TIC_Tech = Bandwidth_Mbps × TIC_Rate_configured
VAT_Due = Annual_Revenue × VAT_Rate_configured
License_Fee = per_license_type_configured
EFR_Gap = Total_Due − Amount_Declared
📁 Data Sources — How Each ISP Category is Identified
🔌 Source 1 — BGP Routing Table (auto-detected or uploaded)
NOCTYRA360™ queries the national IXP routing table or accepts a BGP dump export.
Format: MRT/BIRD dump, plain text, or CSV with AS number, AS name, IP prefix, peer capacity.
→ Identifies ALL ISPs announcing IP blocks — licensed and unlicensed
📊 Source 2 — RADIUS Accounting Logs (upload per ISP)
Licensed ISPs export their AAA/RADIUS session logs. Upload CSV or syslog format.
NOCTYRA360™ aggregates: total sessions, total data volume, unique subscribers, revenue per period.
→ Computes actual bandwidth consumption for TIC-TECH tax base
🛰 Source 3 — VSAT Terminal Registration File (satellite operators)
Spectrum authority exports VSAT terminal registration data. Upload to NOCTYRA360™.
Fields: terminal ID, operator name, GPS coordinates, frequency band, licensed capacity.
→ Identifies every satellite terminal — cross-referenced with declared subscribers
🔎 Source 4 — DNS Query Analysis (from IXP resolver or upload)
DNS query logs from the national IXP resolver identify which foreign platforms are actively serving local users.
Format: syslog, PCAP summary, or DNS query export (domain, query count, unique resolvers).
→ Identifies non-resident platforms (Netflix, Google, Meta, TikTok) operating locally
📥 Source 5 — Licensed ISP Registry (from Telecom Regulator)
Upload the official licensed ISP registry from the regulator. CSV or XLSX.
Fields: ISP name, license number, license type, AS number, license status, expiry date.
→ Any ISP detected in BGP but not in this registry = unlicensed → immediate flag
⚖ The 5 ISP Categories — Fiscal Treatment and Detection Method
#CategoryHow DetectedTax BaseTaxes AppliedNOCTYRA360™ Infrastructure
1 Resident Licensed ISP BGP AS matched in licensed registry · License number verified Bandwidth purchased (Mbps) + Annual revenue TIC-TECH on bandwidth · VAT on revenue · Annual license fee RADIUS logs uploaded monthly · Bandwidth cross-checked against IXP records · Declaration compared automatically
2 Resident Unlicensed ISP BGP AS detected but NOT in licensed registry · WiFi SSID scan Estimated revenue from IP block size + subscriber density TIC-TECH · VAT · License fee penalty · Regulatory fine BGP scan → unlicensed AS flagged → GPS location estimated from IP geolocation → formal notice generated
3 Non-Resident Foreign Platform DNS query volume to foreign domains · IXP traffic analysis · App store rankings Local subscriber revenue (ARPU × subscriber count × 12) NR-VAT on local revenue (BEPS Action 1) · Digital services tax DNS logs identify platform presence · Subscriber count estimated from traffic volume · NR-VAT computed and formal registration demand issued
4 Satellite / VSAT Operator VSAT terminal registration file · Frequency license records · Satellite beacon scan Subscriber count × monthly subscription price × 12 ISP tax on bandwidth · VAT on subscriptions · Spectrum usage fee VSAT terminal count from spectrum authority · Subscriber list cross-referenced · Revenue estimated · Tax computed per configured rates
5 WiFi Reseller / Hotspot WiFi SSID scan · Agent reports · Airtime resale CDR patterns Resale revenue (bandwidth purchased × markup) Reseller license fee · TIC-TECH on purchased bandwidth Network probe deployed at public locations identifies active SSIDs · Operator cross-referenced with licensed reseller registry · Bandwidth purchase volume traced from upstream ISP CDRs
⚔ Why ISP Detection is Independent of Operator Cooperation
BGP routing table entries are public records — any network-connected device in the world can query them. An ISP cannot hide its BGP presence without ceasing to operate.

DNS query volumes at the national IXP are recorded by the national internet infrastructure — not by the operator being assessed.

VSAT terminal registrations are held by the spectrum authority — an independent regulatory body.
An ISP that refuses to submit RADIUS logs or financial declarations can still be assessed because:

● Its IP blocks are visible in the BGP table → bandwidth estimated
● Its subscriber count is estimated from IP block size and DNS volumes
● Its revenue is computed from estimated subscribers × market ARPU

NOCTYRA360™ produces a legally defensible assessment even when the operator refuses to cooperate.

or bandwidth

📋 What is Recorded at Each Step — Full Traceability
① DETECTION RECORD
Detection method (BGP/DNS/VSAT/Registry/Probe) · AS number · IP blocks announced · Bandwidth capacity · Detection timestamp · Confidence level
② CLASSIFICATION RECORD
Category assigned (1–5) · Registry match result · License number (if found) · License status · Classification rationale documented
③ REVENUE ESTIMATION RECORD
Bandwidth Mbps (from RADIUS or IXP) · Subscriber count (from RADIUS or estimated) · ARPU source · Annual revenue estimated · Confidence interval ±%
④ TAX COMPUTATION RECORD
TIC-TECH = Bandwidth × rate. VAT = Revenue × rate. License fee per type. Rate version applied. All rates from configured Tax Rules.
⑤ CERTIFICATION & NOTICE RECORD
EFR = Computed − Declared · Gap · Notice reference number · ISO 8601 timestamp · SHA-256 report hash · 30-day response deadline
📊 Tax Formulas by ISP Category
// Resident Licensed ISP:
TIC_Tech = Bandwidth_Mbps × TIC_Rate
VAT_Due = Annual_Revenue × VAT_Rate
// Non-Resident Platform (BEPS Action 1):
Local_Rev = Subscribers × ARPU × FX_Rate × 12
NR_VAT = Local_Rev × NR_VAT_Rate
// Satellite operator:
Sat_Rev = Terminals × Monthly_Price × FX_Rate × 12
Sat_Tax = Sat_Rev × (ISP_Rate + VAT_Rate)
EFR_Gap = Total_Due − Amount_Declared
// All rates from configured Tax Rules — zero hardcoding
AI Detection Scoring — What Triggers Each Level
COMPLIANT — Licensed, declaring correctly
AS in registry · RADIUS logs submitted · Revenue gap below warning threshold · License current · All taxes declared on time
WARNING ⚠️ — Partial compliance
Revenue gap above warning threshold · RADIUS logs incomplete · License expiring · Bandwidth declared below IXP-measured capacity
CRITICAL 🔴 — Action required
AS not in registry (unlicensed) · NR platform with zero declaration · Satellite terminals with no fiscal presence · Revenue gap above critical threshold
⚔ Legal Admissibility — ISP Evidence
BGP routing records are maintained by AFRINIC / RIPE / ARIN — the internet's registration authorities. They are immutable public records.

IXP connection records are maintained by the national internet exchange — a neutral infrastructure body.

VSAT terminal registrations are held by the spectrum authority — a sovereign regulatory body.

None of these records can be altered by the ISP being assessed.

An ISP that refuses to declare is assessed from public infrastructure data — the refusal itself becomes evidence of non-compliance.
📡 CDR Detail — Complete Transaction Record
💳 Mobile Money Detail — Complete Transaction Record
🎮 Gaming & Betting — Complete Transaction Detail
🖥️ Digital Services — Complete Provider & Transaction Detail
🌐 ISP / FAI — Complete Provider & Fiscal Detail
🌏 Roaming & International Traffic — Complete Record Detail
⏱ Expired Recharge — Complete Record & Tax Impact

🔎 Technical Guide
📁 Upload CDR Files — Detection Starts Here
SIM Box detection requires no specialized hardware and no operator cooperation. NOCTYRA360™ identifies SIM Box activity from standard CDR files alone — the same files every operator already produces for billing purposes. Drop the telecom CDR file below and NOCTYRA360™ automatically applies all 5 detection indicators.
📡
Primary — Telecom CDR File
CSV · TXT · ASN.1 — same format as CDR Ingestion module
Columns: timestamp, call_type, orig_msisdn, dest_msisdn, duration, data_mb, revenue, operator_id
✓ All 5 SIM Box indicators extracted automatically
📄
Optional — Interconnect Settlement File
CSV · XLSX — bilateral interconnect settlement records
Columns: partner_operator, traffic_minutes, termination_rate, amount_billed, period
✓ Cross-validates CDR termination volume vs billed settlements
📊 Files Loaded
📡 Telecom CDR FileNo file loaded
📄 Interconnect Settlement FileOptional — not loaded
▶ What NOCTYRA360™ Detects From Your CDR
Indicator 1: SMS/Voice ratio per MSISDN — ratios above 100:1 on international routes = SIM Box signature
Indicator 2: Average call duration — IDD calls below 60 seconds consistently = test call pattern
Indicator 3: Calls under 15 seconds — concentration of sub-15s calls = SIM registration test traffic
Indicator 4: MSISDN activation pattern — long dormancy then sudden high-volume activation = SIM card batch loaded
Indicator 5: Billed vs actual duration ratio — billing inflated vs CDR-recorded duration = revenue manipulation
📊 5 NOCTYRA360™ SIM Box Detection Indicators
#IndicatorObserved ValueNormal ThresholdStatus
1SMS/Voice Ratio — International Roaming859:13–6:1🔴 CRITICAL
2Average roaming call duration45.5s120–180s🔴 ABNORMAL
3Calls <15s (test calls)2 calls at 11.5s< low threshold⚠ SUSPECT
4Sudden activation after silent period19 silent days then activationContinuous🔴 SIM BOX
5Billed/Real Duration RatioUp to 5.2×<1.5×⚠ ABNORMAL
🚨 NOCTYRA360™ Verdict
🔴
SIM Box Confirmed — All 5 indicators converge
The combination of 5 indicators is consistent with one or more active SIM boxes on the analyzed network. Typical loss: a significant portion of incoming international revenue — operator-dependent.

Next step: access to raw SS7 CDRs + interconnect settlement files for definitive confirmation.
💡 What is a SIM Box?
A SIM Box converts incoming international calls (cheap VoIP) into local GSM calls.
Result: international interconnect revenue disappears from CDRs, local traffic inflates artificially, and the operator loses termination revenue it does not declare.
Typical loss: a significant portion of incoming international revenue — operator-dependent.
💡 How NOCTYRA360™ Confirms
1. Raw SS7 CDRs→ Repeated IMEI on hundreds of calls
2. Settlement files→ Undeclared termination
3. Traffic correlation→ Local voice ↑ + intl incoming ↓
4. Radio frequency scan→ Optional field module
5. GPS certified report→ Admissible before regulator
📄 Generate Formal Report
ℹ
This report is a legally admissible document certifying SIM Box detection indicators, suitable for submission to the regulatory authority, tax administration, or court.
🚧 SIM Box & Grey Route Detector — What This Module Does
A SIM Box converts international VoIP calls into local GSM calls using local SIM cards. The result: incoming international traffic is terminated locally at local call rates, the international interconnect revenue disappears from CDRs, and the operator loses the termination fee that should have been declared and taxed. NOCTYRA360™ identifies SIM Box activity from standard CDR patterns alone — without any specialized hardware, without field investigation, and without operator cooperation.
📊 5 Indicators — Extracted From a Single CDR File
All 5 Converge = SIM Box Confirmed
// Suspicious MSISDN extracted from CDR — pattern visible in raw data:
+2348099001122 | 859 SMS / 1 VOICE | avg duration: 45.5s | sub-15s calls: 2 | silent: 19 days then burst | billed/real: 5.2×
1
SMS/Voice Ratio — 859:1 observed vs normal 3–6:1
A SIM Box uses its local SIM cards primarily to terminate voice calls arriving as VoIP. It has almost no reason to send SMS. A ratio above 100:1 on international roaming MSISDNs is the clearest single indicator of SIM Box activity. Normal subscriber behavior: 3–6 SMS per voice call.
2
Average Call Duration — 45.5 seconds vs normal 120–180 seconds
International calls terminated via SIM Box are re-originated as local calls. The VoIP-to-GSM conversion introduces latency and quality issues that cause callers to hang up faster. Average duration under 60 seconds on a roaming MSISDN is a strong SIM Box indicator.
3
Calls Under 15 Seconds — 2 test calls at 11.5 seconds
SIM Box operators test each SIM card by making short calls to verify it is active and not blocked by the operator's anti-fraud system. A cluster of sub-15 second calls on a roaming MSISDN — especially after a dormancy period — is a registration test pattern.
4
Silent Period Then Burst — 19 days dormant then sudden activation
SIM cards in a SIM Box are loaded in batches. They show zero or minimal activity for extended periods while the operator's fraud detection clears them, then suddenly activate with high-volume traffic. This on/off pattern — combined with the other indicators — is definitive.
5
Billed/Real Duration Ratio — 5.2× inflation vs normal 1.0–1.5×
Some SIM Box operators manipulate billing records to inflate declared duration — attempting to reduce the visible per-minute rate while extracting more revenue. NOCTYRA360™ compares CDR-recorded duration against billing records to detect this manipulation.
💰 Why SIM Box is a Fiscal Priority
When a call arrives from abroad as legitimate international traffic, the operator earns an interconnect termination fee — typically the highest-revenue call type in the CDR.

A SIM Box intercepts that call before it reaches the operator's network, terminates it locally using a pre-paid SIM card, and the operator receives nothing. The termination revenue disappears entirely from both CDRs and declarations.

Every minute terminated via SIM Box is a minute of interconnect revenue that was never declared and never taxed.
What NOCTYRA360™ certifies:

● Volume of traffic confirmed as SIM Box (minutes)
● Estimated interconnect revenue lost per period
● Tax due on undeclared termination revenue
● EFR gap — certified as legally admissible

The certified report identifies the suspicious MSISDNs, the detection indicators that triggered each flag, and the computed fiscal gap — ready for submission to the telecom regulator and tax authority.
🔌 Detection Flow — From Raw CDR to Certified SIM Box Report
📡
CDR Ingested
Standard CDR file
Any operator BSS
Any format
⟶
🔎
Pattern Analysis
5 indicators
per MSISDN
per period
⟶
🤖
AI Scoring
Indicators weighted
Confidence 0–100
Per MSISDN
⟶
⚖
EFR Computed
Lost minutes
× interconnect rate
× tax rate
⟶
📋
Certified Report
MSISDN list
Indicators logged
Admissible
📋 What is Recorded — Full Traceability
① CDR INGESTION RECORD
File name · File hash (SHA-256) · Row count · Operator · Period · Ingestion timestamp
② INDICATOR EXTRACTION RECORD
Per MSISDN: SMS/Voice ratio · Avg duration · Sub-15s count · Silent days before burst · Billed/real ratio · Indicator triggered (YES/NO per indicator)
③ AI CONFIDENCE RECORD
Indicators triggered (count of 5) · Weighted confidence score · Classification: SUSPECT / PROBABLE / CONFIRMED · Model version
④ EFR COMPUTATION RECORD
Confirmed SIM Box minutes · Interconnect rate applied (configured) · Lost revenue computed · Tax due on undeclared termination income · Gap certified
⑤ CERTIFICATION RECORD
Suspicious MSISDN list · All 5 indicator values per MSISDN · ISO 8601 report timestamp · SHA-256 report hash · Admissible before regulator and tax authority
📊 EFR Computation Formula
// Per confirmed SIM Box MSISDN:
SB_Minutes = Σ call_duration ÷ 60
Lost_Revenue = SB_Minutes × Interconnect_Rateconfigured
Tax_Due = Lost_Revenue × IDD_Tax_Rateconfigured
EFR_Gap = Tax_Due — (no declaration: operator had zero termination declared)
// All rates from configured Tax Rules — zero hardcoding
Detection Confidence Levels
NORMAL — No SIM Box
0–1 indicators triggered · SMS/Voice ratio normal · Avg duration normal · Continuous activity pattern
SUSPECT ⚠️ — 2–3 Indicators
2 or 3 indicators triggered · Possible SIM Box or anomalous subscriber behavior · Further investigation recommended
CONFIRMED 🔴 — 4–5 Indicators
4 or 5 indicators triggered · SIM Box confirmed · Formal report generated · EFR gap certified · Report admissible before regulator
⚔ Why CDR Evidence is Legally Admissible
The CDR is generated by the operator's own billing system — not by NOCTYRA360™.

The 5 indicators are computed from the operator's own records. The operator cannot dispute data they produced themselves.

The certified report includes the CDR file hash, the indicator values, the computation formulas, and the ISO 8601 timestamp — making every finding fully traceable and reproducible.

An operator challenging the SIM Box finding must explain the anomalous patterns in their own billing records.

📁 Upload MTO Data — Start Here
NOCTYRA360™ analyzes international remittance from MTO transaction files. The tax base is the MTO commission / fee collected on each transfer — not the full transfer amount. Bank SWIFT operations are out of scope. Drop your MTO transaction file below to begin.

Primary — MTO Transaction File
CSV · XLSX · JSON — one row per transfer
Columns: Date · MTO · Corridor · Amount_Sent · Fees · Amount_Received · Currency_Send · Currency_Recv · Status
✓ Computes transfer tax · EFR gap · AML corridor risk per operator
📥
Optional — Registered MTO Registry
CSV · XLSX — list of licensed MTOs from the Central Bank / regulator
Columns: MTO_Name · License_Number · License_Status · Expiry_Date
✓ Identifies unregistered MTOs operating without license

SWIFT wire transfers (bank-to-bank)
Bank account deposits / withdrawals
Standard bank credits / debits
Interbank operations
→ Central Bank / banking supervisor scope
✅ IN SCOPE — Remittance / Digital Transfers
Non-bank Money Transfer Operators (MTO)
Digital platforms (Wave, Wise, Sendwave)
Diaspora transfers (Western Union, MoneyGram)
Mobile-to-mobile cross-border transfers
→ NOCTYRA360™ scope — taxes on fees and volumes
