🌐 International Remittance — What This Module Does
International remittance is one of the most undermonitored revenue categories in telecom and fintech taxation. Money Transfer Operators (MTOs) collect a commission on every transfer — and that commission is taxable. Most MTOs declare transfer volumes but exclude or minimize the fee revenue on which tax is actually due. NOCTYRA360™ extracts every fee, applies the configured tax rate to each corridor, identifies unregistered operators, and certifies the EFR gap — per MTO, per corridor, per period.
📊 A Real MTO Transaction Record — What NOCTYRA360™ Sees
Tax Base = Fee, Not Amount
// Single MTO transaction record:
2026-03-15 | Wave | XOF → EUR | Amount_Sent: 150,000 XOF | Fee: 3,500 XOF | Amount_Received: 146,500 XOF | Status: COMPLETED
Wave MTO Operator — Matched against the licensed MTO registry. If not found → flagged as unregistered → full volume taxable + penalty.
XOF → EUR Corridor — Send/receive country pair. Each corridor has its own configured tax rate. FATF-listed corridors trigger enhanced due diligence automatically.
Fee: 3,500 XOF Tax Base — The MTO commission is the tax base. The full amount (150,000 XOF) is NOT taxed — only the 3,500 XOF fee the MTO keeps. This is the most common error in remittance tax declarations.
150,000 XOF Amount Sent — Used for AML monitoring and volume reporting. Not the tax base unless the country applies a levy on the sent amount — configured separately in Tax Rules.
⚖ What NOCTYRA360™ Computes From This Record
// Per transaction:
Tax_Base = 3,500 XOF (fee)
Transfer_Tax = Tax_Base × configured rate
VAT = Tax_Base × VAT rate
MTO declared: per declaration file
EFR Gap = Tax_Due − Declared
📢 MTO Categories — Scope, Detection and Tax Treatment
MTO TypeKey OperatorsDetectionTax BaseNOCTYRA360™ Action
Digital MTO Wave, Wise, Sendwave API / MTO transaction file upload Fee per transfer Fee extracted per record → tax computed → EFR gap certified per corridor
Traditional MTO Western Union, MoneyGram Agent network declaration + transaction file Commission per transfer Commission cross-referenced vs declared → gap identified → notice generated
Mobile-to-Mobile Orange Money, MTN MoMo, Airtel Money MM CDR cross-border records Transfer fee collected by operator Cross-border MM CDR records filtered → fee extracted → tax applied per corridor
Unregistered MTO Informal hawala networks, unlicensed agents AML pattern detection + field reporting Estimated volume × market fee rate Detected via AML signals → revenue estimated → full tax + penalty → regulatory dossier
⚖ The Key Fiscal Distinction — Always Configured, Never Hardcoded
Standard regime (most countries):

Tax base = MTO fee / commission only
The transfer amount passes through untaxed
The MTO declares and remits tax on fees collected

→ NOCTYRA360™ extracts Fee column and applies configured rate
Amount-based levy (some countries):

A levy applies on Amount_Sent above a configured threshold
Typically a small flat rate or tiered rate per band
Applied in addition to the fee-based tax

→ Configured in Tax Rules — zero hardcoding in NOCTYRA360™

📋 Traceability — What is Recorded at Each Step
① FILE INGESTION RECORD
File name · SHA-256 hash · Row count · MTO operators detected · Period covered · Ingestion timestamp
② REGISTRY VERIFICATION RECORD
Per MTO: license number · status · expiry date · registered YES/NO · if NO → penalty flag activated
③ TAX COMPUTATION RECORD
Per corridor: total fees · tax rate applied · transfer tax due · VAT due · amount-based levy (if configured) · total tax due
④ AML SIGNAL RECORD
Corridor FATF status · structuring patterns detected · smurfing signals · same-sender multiple-beneficiary · volume vs income ratio
⑤ EFR CERTIFICATION RECORD
Tax due per MTO · Tax declared per MTO · EFR gap · ISO 8601 timestamp · SHA-256 report hash · Admissible before tax authority
📊 EFR Computation Formula
// Per MTO, per corridor, per period:
Fee_Total = Σ Fee per completed transfer
Transfer_Tax = Fee_Total × Tax_Rateconfigured
VAT_Due = Fee_Total × VAT_Rateconfigured
Total_Due = Transfer_Tax + VAT_Due + Levyif configured
EFR_Gap = Total_Due − Declared
// All rates from Tax Rules — zero hardcoding
AML Risk Signals — Automatically Flagged
STRUCTURING
Repeated transfers just below the declaration threshold by the same sender — classic tax avoidance pattern
SMURFING
Same sender → multiple different beneficiaries within 24h — amount split to avoid single large transaction flag
FATF CORRIDOR
Transfer to/from FATF grey or black-listed country → enhanced due diligence triggered automatically

📁 Upload Crypto Data — Start Here
Crypto taxation requires data from three independent sources: exchange transaction records, on-chain blockchain data, and payment flows (Mobile Money or bank transfers used to buy/sell crypto). NOCTYRA360™ cross-references all three to detect undeclared capital gains, unregistered exchanges, and USDT used as unregulated remittance.
₿
Source 1 — Exchange Transaction Records
CSV · XLSX — from licensed exchanges under regulatory mandate
Columns: Date · User_ID · Asset · Type (buy/sell/transfer) · Amount · Price · Fee · Gain_Loss
✓ Capital gains computed per transaction per user
🔌
Source 2 — On-Chain Blockchain Data
CSV · JSON — wallet addresses and transaction hashes
Columns: Wallet_Address · TxHash · Asset · Amount · Timestamp · Direction (in/out)
✓ Identifies wallets transacting in the country — even on DEX
💳
Source 3 — Mobile Money / Bank Flows
CSV — MM CDR or bank transfer records to/from crypto platforms
Columns: Date · Sender · Receiver · Amount · Platform · Direction
✓ Identifies USDT used as unregulated remittance
📊 Files Loaded
₿ Exchange Transaction RecordsNo file loaded
🔌 On-Chain Blockchain DataNo file loaded
💳 Mobile Money / Bank FlowsNo file loaded
▶ What NOCTYRA360™ Computes After Upload
Exchange file → Capital gains per user → Tax due at configured rate → Declared vs computed gap → Unregistered exchange detection
Blockchain file → Local wallets identified → DEX activity detected → Unreported transactions flagged → Cross-reference with exchange records
MM/Bank file → Crypto purchase flows identified → USDT remittance volume computed → MTO substitution gap certified → AML signals scored
🔄 Crypto Exchange Registry — Licensed vs Unlicensed
Every exchange operating in the country must be registered with the central bank or financial regulator. NOCTYRA360™ cross-references detected exchanges against the registry and flags unlicensed operators.

Asset ClassExamplesTax EventTax BaseRateNOCTYRA360™ Action
Proof-of-WorkBitcoin (BTC), LitecoinSale, exchange, paymentCapital gain (sell price − cost basis)ConfiguredGain computed per transaction · FIFO/LIFO per settings
StablecoinsUSDT, USDC, BUSDTransfer / remittance useTransfer amount (if used as MTO substitute)ConfiguredMM-like flows detected → MTO substitution flagged → transfer tax applied
Smart Contract TokensETH, BNB, MATICSale, staking reward, DeFi yieldGain on sale + staking incomeConfiguredStaking rewards treated as income · DeFi yield taxed at income rate
NFTsDigital art, gaming assetsSale of NFTSale price − creation/purchase costConfiguredOn-chain NFT sales detected · Creator royalties tracked

DeFi YieldLiquidity pools, lendingYield receivedYield amount at market priceConfiguredDeFi protocol interactions detected · Yield computed as taxable income

🚨 AML / FATF Signals — Crypto-Specific
Why crypto AML is different:

Traditional AML tracks bank accounts. Crypto AML tracks wallet addresses. A single user may operate hundreds of wallets. NOCTYRA360™ uses transaction graph analysis to cluster wallets and identify the beneficial owner behind complex transaction patterns.
CHAIN PEELING
Large amount sent through many small sequential transactions — classic crypto layering to obscure origin
MIXER / TUMBLER USE
Transactions routed through mixing services — breaks traceability chain · FATF red flag
DARK MARKET WALLETS
Wallet address appears on OFAC/EU/UN sanctions lists or known dark market databases
USDT REMITTANCE SUBSTITUTION
USDT transfers mirror remittance corridor patterns — bypassing MTO licensing and transfer tax obligations
⚠ EFR Gap — Crypto Sector Certified Assessment
Capital Gains EFR
—
Unlicensed Exchange EFR
—
USDT MTO EFR
—
Total Crypto EFR
—

Upload exchange data then click AI Report to generate the full crypto compliance analysis.

₿ Crypto & Digital Assets — What This Module Does
Cryptocurrency taxation is the fastest-growing compliance gap in developing economies. NOCTYRA360™ addresses three distinct crypto tax problems simultaneously: capital gains on exchange-traded crypto, income tax on mining and staking rewards, and MTO substitution — USDT used to transfer money across borders without going through a licensed MTO and without paying the applicable transfer tax.
📊 A Real Exchange Transaction Record — What NOCTYRA360™ Sees
Capital Gains Computed Per Trade
// Exchange transaction record — provided under regulatory mandate:
2026-03-15T14:22:31Z | User: USR-448821 | Asset: BTC | Type: SELL | Amount: 0.5 BTC | Price: $31,200 | Cost_Basis: $18,500 | Gain: $6,350
Type: SELL Taxable event — A sale of crypto for fiat currency triggers capital gains tax. Transfers between wallets do NOT trigger tax unless between unrelated parties.
Gain: $6,350 Capital gain = Tax base — Sell price ($31,200 × 0.5) minus cost basis ($18,500 × 0.5) = $6,350 gain. Tax is due on the gain, not the full sale amount. NOCTYRA360™ tracks cost basis per FIFO or LIFO as configured.
⚖ Tax Computed
Gain = $6,350
Tax = $6,350 × configured rate
Declared: per user declaration
EFR = Tax_Due − Declared
🔌 The USDT Remittance Problem — Unique to Developing Markets
In many developing economies, USDT (Tether) has become the de facto remittance currency. A sender buys USDT with Mobile Money, sends it to a wallet abroad, and the receiver sells it for local currency. No MTO license. No transfer tax. No AML oversight.
NOCTYRA360™ detects this by cross-referencing MM flows to crypto platforms against standard remittance corridor patterns. When USDT transfers match remittance behavior (same corridors, same timing, same amounts), they are flagged as MTO substitution and the applicable transfer tax is computed on the full amount.
🔌 Data Flow — From Exchange Records to Certified Crypto EFR
📁
Ingest
Exchange file
+ Chain data
+ MM flows
⟶
🔄
Cross-Ref
Exchange vs
on-chain vs
MM flows

+ income
+ MTO tax

🔴
EFR Gap
Per user
Per asset
Certified
⟶
📋
Report
Admissible
Per user
Hashed
📊 EFR Formula — Capital Gains
// Per taxable event:
Gain = Sell_Price − Cost_Basis
Tax_Due = Gain × CGT_Rateconfigured
// Per staking / mining event:
Income_Tax = FMV_at_Receipt × Income_Rateconfigured
// USDT remittance substitution:
Transfer_Tax = USDT_Amount × MTO_Rateconfigured
EFR_Gap = Total_Due − Declared
// All rates from Tax Rules — zero hardcoding
📋 Traceability Chain
① DATA INGESTION
File name · SHA-256 hash · Record count · Exchange name · Period covered · Ingestion timestamp
② COST BASIS RECORD
Accounting method (FIFO/LIFO/configured) · Cost basis per lot · Holding period · Asset type
③ TAX COMPUTATION
Gain per event · Rate applied · Tax type (CGT/income/MTO) · Currency · FX rate
④ EFR CERTIFICATION
Total due · Declared · Gap · ISO 8601 timestamp · SHA-256 report hash · Admissible
📺 Pay-TV & Subscription Broadcasting
CAS Database · Certified Subscribers · Canal+ Horizon · StarTimes · DTH Satellite · DTT · IPTV · Subscription Tax · Customs on Decoders · EFR Gap

📖 About This Module
🔎 Technical Guide
📁 Upload Pay-TV Data — Start Here
Pay-TV taxation starts from the CAS (Conditional Access System) — the most reliable source of subscriber count in the industry. The operator cannot falsify the CAS without cutting signal to their own subscribers. Drop the CAS export and the operator tax declaration below.
📺
Source 1 — CAS Database Export
CSV · XLSX — provided by operator under regulatory mandate
Columns: Decoder_ID · Smart_Card · Package · Activation_Date · Status · Operator
⭐ Gold Standard — operator cannot manipulate
📄
Source 2 — Operator Tax Declaration
CSV · XLSX · PDF — declared subscriber count and revenue
Columns: Period · Subscribers_Declared · Revenue_Declared · Tax_Paid · Package

🤖 AI Analysis — Pay-TV Compliance Report
ℹ
Upload CAS and declaration files then click AI Report to generate the full Pay-TV compliance analysis.
Waiting for analysis...
📺 Pay-TV & Broadcasting — What This Module Does
Pay-TV operators collect subscription revenue from every active decoder on their network. The most common fraud: declaring fewer active subscribers than the CAS system records. NOCTYRA360™ cross-references the CAS database against the declared subscriber count and computes the certified EFR gap per operator, per package, per period. The CAS is the irrefutable evidence — the operator cannot manipulate it without cutting signal to subscribers they claim do not exist.
📊 A Real CAS Database Record — What It Proves
Operator Cannot Falsify This
// CAS export record — generated by the conditional access system:
DECODER_ID: DEC-2048831 | Smart_Card: SC-9914572 | Package: PREMIUM | Activation: 2024-08-12 | Status: ACTIVE | Operator: Canal+ Horizon
DECODER_ID Unique decoder identifier — Every physical decoder is registered. The CAS knows exactly how many are active at any moment.
Smart_Card Smart card number — Each active card = one paying subscriber. Cards cannot be active without payment.
Package: PREMIUM Subscription package — CAS count × ARPU = revenue that must have been collected. Any gap to declared revenue = undeclared income.
Status: ACTIVE Active status — Only ACTIVE decoders are counted. Suspended or cancelled decoders excluded. NOCTYRA360™ uses only ACTIVE records for the tax base.
⚖ What NOCTYRA360™ Computes From CAS
// Per package, per period:
Active_Decoders = COUNT(Status=ACTIVE)
Revenue_CAS = Decoders × ARPU_package
Tax_Due = Revenue_CAS × Tax_Rateconfigured
EFR_Gap = Tax_Due − Tax_Declared
📺 Pay-TV Service Types — Detection and Tax Treatment
Service TypeKey OperatorsDetection MethodTax BaseNOCTYRA360™ Action
DTH SatelliteCanal+ Horizon, StarTimes, Azam TVCAS database · Smart card registryActive decoders × monthly ARPUCAS count vs declared → gap certified → EFR per package
DTT TerrestrialState broadcaster, local DTT operatorsSet-top box registry · Spectrum licenseLicense fee + subscription revenueLicensed decoder count vs declared → gap computed
IPTVTelecom operators with IPTV offeringSubscriber database · Billing recordsActive subscriptions × monthly ARPUIPTV subscribers cross-referenced with CAS → EFR computed
Cable TVLocal cable operatorsConnection registry · Physical infrastructureConnected households × tariffPhysical connections mapped vs declared → gap certified
⭐ Why CAS is the Gold Standard
The CAS manages signal encryption for every decoder. A decoder cannot receive signal unless the CAS authorizes it. The CAS cannot authorize without payment being processed. An operator claiming 50,000 subscribers but with 80,000 active CAS decoders has a 30,000-decoder gap that cannot be explained by technical error.
The operator cannot manipulate the CAS without:

● Cutting signal to subscribers they claim do not exist
● Invalidating smart cards mid-subscription
● Creating fraudulent entries their own billing contradicts

The CAS export is the Pay-TV equivalent of the CDR file — the irrefutable production record.
🔌 Data Flow — From CAS Export to Certified EFR Report
📺
CAS Export
Decoder list
Per operator
Under mandate
⟶
👥
Count Active
Status=ACTIVE
Per package

× tax rate

Cross-Check
vs declared
subscribers
⟶
🔴
EFR Gap
Certified
Per operator
⟶
📋
Report
Admissible
Hashed
📊 EFR Formula
// Per operator, per package, per period:
CAS_Subs = COUNT(decoder WHERE status=ACTIVE)
Revenue_CAS = CAS_Subs × ARPU_package
Tax_Due = Revenue_CAS × Tax_Rateconfigured
EFR_Gap = Tax_Due − Tax_Declared
// All rates from Tax Rules — zero hardcoding
📋 Traceability Chain
① CAS INGESTION
File name · SHA-256 hash · Total rows · Active count · Period · Timestamp
② SUBSCRIBER COUNT
Active decoders per package · ARPU per package · Revenue computed · Period
③ TAX COMPUTATION
Subscription tax + VAT + content levy · Configured rates applied
④ EFR CERTIFICATION
Gap per operator · ISO 8601 timestamp · SHA-256 hash · Admissible

📁 Upload Historical Data — Train the AI Model
The Predictive AI Engine learns from historical EFR data across all modules. The more historical periods you upload, the more accurate the 12-month forecast. Minimum: 3 periods. Optimal: 24+ months of data across all operators and sectors.
📈
Historical EFR Data
CSV · XLSX — export from any NOCTYRA360™ module's EFR Gap tab
Columns: Period · Module · Operator · EFR_Gap · Tax_Declared · Tax_Due · Confidence
✓ Trains the forecast model across all sectors
🌐
Optional — Macro Indicators
CSV — GDP growth, subscriber growth, inflation, exchange rates
Columns: Period · GDP_Growth · Subscriber_Growth · Inflation · FX_Rate
✓ Improves forecast accuracy with external economic context
📊 Files Loaded
📈 Historical EFR DataNo file loaded
🌐 Macro IndicatorsOptional — not loaded
▶ What the AI Produces
EFR Forecast → 12-month projection per module and per operator · Upper and lower confidence bounds · CAGR trend line
Risk Scores → Each operator scored 0–100 on non-compliance risk · Ranked by expected EFR gap · Pre-audit priority list generated
Early Alerts → Operators whose declared revenue deviates from predicted trajectory → 30-day advance warning before formal audit

🚨 Operator Risk Scoring — AI-Ranked Non-Compliance Priority
Each operator is scored on non-compliance risk based on: historical EFR gap patterns, declaration variability, sector exposure, and behavioral anomalies. Higher score = higher audit priority.
Critical Risk (80–100)
0
Immediate audit recommended
High Risk (60–79)
0
Priority queue
Medium Risk (40–59)
0
Enhanced monitoring
Low Risk (0–39)
0
Standard monitoring
OperatorModuleRisk ScorePrimary DriverTrendRecommendation
Run AI forecast to generate risk scores
🔔 Pre-Declaration Alerts — 30-Day Early Warning System
When an operator's observed metrics deviate significantly from the AI's expected trajectory, an alert is generated 30 days before the declaration period — giving time to request clarification before formal audit is required.
What triggers a pre-declaration alert:

● Subscriber count lower than forecast by more than configured threshold
● Revenue declared below expected trajectory
● CDR volume anomaly vs previous periods
● New CDR pattern matching known evasion signatures
What happens with an alert:

● Operator receives automated clarification request
● 15-day window to provide explanation
● If no satisfactory response → formal audit triggered
● All alerts logged with ISO 8601 timestamp
Alert IDOperatorModuleAnomaly DetectedExpectedObservedAlert DateStatus
No pre-declaration alerts — run forecast to monitor
🎯 Scenario Analysis — Legislative Impact Simulation
Model the fiscal impact of proposed tax rate changes, new sector coverage, or regulatory reforms before they are enacted. The AI computes the expected change in EFR collection under each scenario.
⚙ Configure Scenario
📈 Scenario Results
Configure and run a scenario to see projected impact
🤖 AI Full Intelligence Report — NOCTYRA360™ Predictive Analysis
ℹ
Upload historical data, run forecast, then click AI Report for the full 12-month compliance intelligence briefing.
Waiting for forecast data...
🤖 Predictive AI Engine — What This Module Does
The Predictive AI Engine transforms NOCTYRA360™ from a reactive audit tool into a proactive compliance intelligence system. Instead of discovering EFR gaps after declaration periods close, the AI forecasts which operators are likely to under-declare — 30 days in advance — allowing the tax authority to request clarification before the formal audit cycle begins. This reduces dispute costs, accelerates collection, and shifts the compliance dynamic from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.
📈 What the AI Learns From
Data SourceWhat It Teaches the AIForecast Impact
Historical EFR GapsWhich operators consistently under-declare and by how muchPrimary forecast driver — operator behavioral baseline
CDR Volume TrendsSeasonal patterns, subscriber growth, traffic shiftsCalibrates revenue expectations per period
Declaration VariabilityOperators that declare inconsistently vs stablyRisk score driver — high variability = high risk
Sector EFR PatternsWhich sectors have systemic under-declarationCross-sector calibration of expected compliance rates
Macro IndicatorsGDP, inflation, subscriber growth ratesExternal context for revenue trajectory adjustments
🎯 Why Predictive Compliance Changes Everything
Traditional approach (reactive):

Operator declares → Tax authority reviews → Gap detected → Audit initiated → Operator disputes → 18 months of process → Collection (maybe)

Cost: high. Speed: slow. Recovery rate: unpredictable.
NOCTYRA360™ predictive approach:

AI detects trajectory anomaly 30 days early → Clarification request sent → Operator self-corrects declaration → Correct tax collected at declaration time

Cost: minimal. Speed: immediate. Recovery rate: near 100% on alerted operators.
🔌 AI Model Architecture — How the Forecast Works
📈
Historical Data
EFR per period
All modules
All operators
⟶
🤖

Trend · Seasonality
Operator baseline
Anomaly flags

+ ML regression
+ confidence bands

Per operator

Auto-notification
Admissible
📋 Risk Score Formula
// Per operator:
Risk_Score = (
EFR_Trend_Weight × EFR_CAGR +
Variability_Weight × Decl_Variance +
Anomaly_Weight × Anomaly_Score
) × 100
// Weights configured in Settings · Score 0–100
// 80+ = Critical · 60-79 = High · 40-59 = Medium · 0-39 = Low
📋 Forecast Accuracy Metrics
HIGH CONFIDENCE — 24+ months training data
Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) typically below 8% · Confidence bands narrow · Operator patterns well established
MEDIUM CONFIDENCE — 6–23 months
MAPE typically 8–18% · Seasonal patterns not fully captured · Directional forecast reliable · Point estimates approximate
INDICATIVE — 3–5 months
MAPE 18–35% · Trend direction reliable · Risk scores directionally accurate · Increase training data for operational use

🔎 Technical Guide
📁 Upload Source Files — Terminal Control
NOCTYRA360™ cross-references 5 data sources to identify every active terminal on the network, verify customs compliance, detect cloned or counterfeit IMEIs, and certify the fiscal loss. No single source is sufficient — the cross-referencing produces the proof.
📡
Source 1 — Operator CDR Files
CSV · XLSX · JSON
Key columns: IMEI · MSISDN · Timestamp · Operator · Type
✓ Every CDR contains the IMEI of the terminal used
📥
Source 2 — Customs Database
CSV · XLSX — Official import declarations
Columns: IMEI · Model · Importer · Date · Value · Duties Paid
✓ Official source — National Customs authority
🌐
Source 3 — GSMA TAC Database
CSV · JSON — GSMA International Type Allocation Code
Columns: TAC · Manufacturer · Model · Approved · Origin
✓ Manufacturer identification + counterfeiting detection
🚨
Source 4 — National Blacklist & Interpol
CSV · TXT — Stolen, fraudulent, reported IMEIs
Columns: IMEI · Reason · Report Date · Operator · Status
✓ Interpol I-CHECKIT cross-check if available
✅
Source 5 — Regulator Type Approval Registry
CSV · XLSX — Devices approved by the national regulator
Columns: TAC/Model · Manufacturer · Approval Date · Certificate
✓ Any terminal absent = not approved = illegal
📊 Files Loaded
📡 Operator CDR FilesNo file loaded
📥 Customs DatabaseNo file loaded
🌐 GSMA TAC DatabaseNo file loaded
🚨 National BlacklistNo file loaded
✅ Regulator Type ApprovalNo file loaded
▶ What NOCTYRA360™ Computes After Upload
CDR + Customs → Every active IMEI checked against customs database → Not found = undeclared terminal → Duties + VAT computed
CDR + GSMA TAC → First 8 digits of IMEI identify the manufacturer → Unknown TAC = suspect or counterfeit IMEI
CDR + Blacklist → Active IMEI but blacklisted = stolen or fraudulent terminal → Block + operator alert
Duplicate IMEI → Same IMEI on 2 networks simultaneously = cloning = serious network fraud
📲 National Terminal Registry — Complete View
All IMEIs detected on the network, classified by customs and regulatory compliance status.

—
Seen on CDR period
Customs Declared
—

Undeclared

Customs fraud
Unknown Status

Verification in progress
IMEITAC (Manufacturer)ModelOperator(s) MSISDNFirst DetectedLast Activity Customs DeclaredType ApprovedBlacklistRisk ScoreStatus
Load Demo or upload CDR + Customs files to populate the registry
📥 Undeclared Terminals — Customs Fiscal Fraud
A terminal is undeclared when it is active on the network but absent from the import customs database. NOCTYRA360™ computes the exact fiscal loss per identified terminal.
Undeclared Terminals
—
Active IMEIs outside customs
Estimated Market Value
—
Average price × quantity
Unpaid Customs Duties
—
Duty rate × value
Unpaid Import VAT
—
VAT × market value
⚖ Customs EFR Calculation Parameters
// EFR per undeclared terminal:
Value = $150
Duties = Value × Rate = $37.50
VAT = Value × VAT = $30.00
Penalty = Subtotal × Pen = $33.75
EFR/terminal = $101.25
📊 Non-Compliant Importer Profiles
🔴 MASS IMPORTER — Operator or Wholesaler
Volume >500 undeclared terminals · Consecutive IMEI batch · Short activation period · Clear commercial profile
⚠ REGULAR IMPORTER — Informal Reseller
50–500 terminals · Activations spread over weeks · Street vendor or informal shop
👤 RECURRING INDIVIDUAL — Frequent Traveler
5–50 terminals · Activations after international travel · Repetitive individual profile
👤 OCCASIONAL INDIVIDUAL — Personal Use
1–4 terminals · Single activation · Probably personal use
🔄 IMEI Cloning Detection — Serious Network Fraud
A cloned IMEI is an identifier illegally copied onto multiple devices. When two terminals with the same IMEI are active simultaneously on the network, NOCTYRA360™ detects it immediately.
Cloned IMEIs
—
Simultaneous double active
Terminals Involved
—
Original + copies
Suspect IMEIs (Sequential)
—
Consecutive IMEI batch
Invalid IMEIs (Luhn fail)
—
Fraudulently generated
🔌 Cloning Detection Methods
1
Simultaneous Double Active — Same IMEI in 2 different CDRs within 5 minutes · Geographically incompatible → Absolute proof of cloning
2
Massive Sequential IMEIs — Batch of consecutive IMEIs activated simultaneously → Fraudulent IMEI production
3
Luhn Check Failure — Luhn algorithm validates every legitimate IMEI · Invalid IMEI = fraudulently generated → Certain counterfeiting
4
TAC Not Recognized by GSMA — First 8 digits absent from GSMA database = fictitious manufacturer → Counterfeit device
5
IMEI 000000000000000 — Null or repeated IMEI = deliberately masked terminal · Common in SIM Boxes and hacked terminals
🚨 National IMEI Blacklist — Centralized Management
The national blacklist centralizes all IMEIs to block on the network: stolen, counterfeit, cloned, or security-risk terminals. NOCTYRA360™ compares every active IMEI in real time against this list.
IMEIs on Blacklist
—
Still Active on Network
—
Blocked by Operators
—
Added This Month
—
🚨 Add IMEI to Blacklist
📋 Multi-Operator Blocking Protocol
1
NOCTYRA360™ Detection — IMEI identified as stolen, cloned or fraudulent
2
Blacklist Addition — IMEI added to centralized list with certified timestamp
3
Operator Notification — Automatic alert to all operators · Max 24h for blocking
4
Network Blocking — Operator blocks IMEI · Terminal can no longer register
5
Confirmation & Report — Operator confirms · NOCTYRA360™ verifies on next CDR
✅ Terminal Type Approval
Every terminal sold or used on the national network must be approved by the telecom regulator. Type approval guarantees the device meets technical standards and protects network integrity. NOCTYRA360™ cross-references every TAC detected on the network against the approval registry.
Approved Models
—
Non-Approved Models
—
Expired Approval
—
Manufacturers Represented
—
ℹ
A recognized GSMA TAC but absent from local registry = imported without type approval = regulatory violation. A TAC absent from both GSMA and local registry = counterfeit device = immediate blocking recommended.
⚠ Certified Customs EFR — Terminal Fiscal Loss
The Customs EFR consolidates all fiscal losses identified on terminals: unpaid customs duties, uncollected import VAT, fraud penalties. Every line is traced back to the source IMEI — the report is legally admissible.
Uncollected Customs Duties
—
Uncollected Import VAT
—
Fraud Penalties
—
Total Certified EFR
—

OperatorUndeclared IMEIsCustoms DutiesImport VATPenaltyEFR Total
Load Demo data to see EFR breakdown
🤖 AI Analysis — NOCTYRA360™ Terminal Control Report
ℹ
The AI analyzes the entire IMEI registry to identify fraud patterns, informal distribution networks, suspicious activation frequencies, and produces a certified summary report for the regulator and Customs authority.
Load source data and click AI Analysis to start...
📲 IMEI & Terminal Control — What This Module Does
This module tackles one of the most invisible but massive frauds in African telecoms: undeclared importation of mobile terminals. Thousands of phones enter every month in traveler luggage, under-declared containers, or through informal distribution networks. The State loses customs duties, import VAT, and bears the costs of counterfeit terminals that degrade network quality and cause security issues. NOCTYRA360™ solves this by cross-referencing CDRs (which contain IMEIs) against the customs database — every discrepancy is a proven and quantified fraud.
📊 The 3 Problems This Module Solves
① Undeclared Importation
Individuals bringing 5-20 phones in luggage · Operators under-declaring imported batches · Informal resellers importing by container · State loses customs duties + import VAT on every device
② Counterfeit & Cloned Terminals
IMEIs duplicated on pirated devices · Invalid GSMA TACs on counterfeits · Network interference from non-standard devices · Network quality degradation + security issues
③ Stolen Terminals Back in Circulation
Stolen phones reactivated due to lack of national blacklist · No inter-operator communication on stolen IMEIs · Encouragement of mobile theft + user insecurity
📊 EFR Potential — By Country Type
MarketEstimated Undeclared Terminals/YearAverage ValueEstimated Customs EFRDetection Source
Large market (DRC, Ethiopia)500,000 – 1,500,000$120 – $200$36M – $135M/yearCDR + Customs
Medium market (Madagascar, Tanzania)100,000 – 500,000$100 – $150$10M – $45M/yearCDR + Customs
Small market (Benin, Mali)20,000 – 100,000$80 – $120$1.5M – $9M/yearCDR + Customs

CDR File
IMEI in every record
⟶

Extract
TAC extracted
Luhn verified
⟶

In database?
YES=Declared
NO=Fraud

NO=Counterfeit

YES=Block
⟶

EFR

+Penalty

📋
Report
SHA-256 hashed
Timestamped
Admissible
⚔ Legal Admissibility — Proof Basis
CDRs are production records automatically generated by operator billing systems.
The customs database is an official State document.
The GSMA TAC database is a public international registry.

An operator or importer contesting the NOCTYRA360™ report must contest one of these 3 registries — all of independent origin.

This triple corroboration makes the NOCTYRA360™ M11 report the strongest proof tool available for terminal control.

Natural Language Query Engine

Type anything in plain language — date, operator, phone number, service, time range, amount. NOCTYRA360™ finds it instantly.

Query Engine Ready
Type any search above or click an example. Supports: dates, times, operators, phone numbers, amounts, services, anomaly types.
Date / TimeMSISDNOperator Service Revenue HT Tax TTC Status
What the Query Engine Understands
📅 Dates March 15, 3/15/2026, Q1 2026, last month, this week
🕐 Times after 22h, between 14h-16h, night hours, morning
📡 Operators Telecel, Orange, all operators, by name
📱 Phone numbers +236720XXXXX, any MSISDN format
💰 Amounts above 500,000 XOF, EFR gap, revenue HT/TTC
⚠ Anomalies score above 80, SIM Box, IMEI invalid, duplicate
🌎 Services voice, SMS, data, Mobile Money, roaming
📊 Reports EFR total, monthly revenue, operator comparison

About NOCTYRA360™ V12

NOCTYRA360™ is Africa’s first comprehensive Digital Economy Fiscal Intelligence Platform, developed by Connect Now USA LLC (Tempe, Arizona). It monitors, analyses, and certifies the complete digital economy of a sovereign nation — from Voice CDR to IMEI device registry — across 12 fully integrated modules, zero hardcoded values, and one unified system deployable in any of 54 African countries.

Every EFR report generated by NOCTYRA360™ carries a SHA-256 cryptographic hash of its source data, making it legally admissible before any tax authority, regulatory body, or court of law. The platform operates on a zero-cost-to-government model — governments pay nothing upfront and nothing unless revenues are recovered.

V12 adds four critical platform layers on top of the battle-tested V11 M11 analysis engines: full country configuration, a 46-type certified report catalog, a natural language query engine, and M12 Supervision & Omission Control — making it the most complete fiscal intelligence system available in Africa today.

V12 New Features
NEW
Phase 1 — Configuration Layer
Zero hardcoded values. Country, currency, operators, taxes, modules — all configurable. Saved to localStorage, read by all platform modules.
NEW
Phase 2 — Report Catalog — 46 Types
All 46 certified report types across 8 categories. Filter by audience, expand for full description, content sheets, and build queue.
NEW
Phase 3 — Natural Language Query Engine
Type any question in plain language. Parses dates, times, operators, MSISDNs, amounts, anomaly types. Returns filtered CDR records instantly.
NEW
Phase 4 — Supervision & Omission Control
Log test transactions every 30 minutes. 24-hour completeness grid. Automatic omission detection and SHA-256 certified omission certificates.
+9
9 Requested Report Types Integrated
All 9 specifically requested reports are tagged YOUR REQUEST in the catalog. Ready for generation in the next analysis session.
100%
Country-Neutral Architecture
Configure for any of 54 African nations. Language, currency, tax framework, operators, regulatory bodies — all set in Configuration. No code changes required.
